Are better together, we can be ourselves, be inclusive, see more good in others, and work collectively to build for the long term
What we offer

In line with our Fair Pay Charter, we offer a competitive salary and benefits to support your mental, physical, financial and social wellbeing.

Core bank funding for retirement savings, medical and life insurance, with flexible and voluntary benefits available in some locations.
Time-off including annual leave, parental/maternity (20 weeks), sabbatical (12 months maximum) and volunteering leave (3 days), along with minimum global standards for annual and public holiday, which is combined to 30 days minimum.
Flexible working options based around home and office locations, with flexible working patterns.
Proactive wellbeing support through Unmind, a market-leading digital wellbeing platform, development courses for resilience and other human skills, global Employee Assistance Programme, sick leave, mental health first-aiders and all sorts of self-help toolkits
A continuous learning culture to support your growth, with opportunities to reskill and upskill and access to physical, virtual and digital learning.
Being part of an inclusive and values driven organisation, one that embraces and celebrates our unique diversity, across our teams, business functions and geographies - everyone feels respected and can realise their full potential.
description.html:
Requisition Number: 53446
Job Location: Hyderabad, IND
Global Grade: Band 6
Work Type: Office Working
Employment Type: Permanent
Posting Start Date: 04/05/2026
Posting End Date: 20/07/2026
:
Job Summary

Business Performance

Description:
  • Achieve the Month-on-Month targets as per the Performance scorecard.
  • To ensure timely MIS with accuracy of data
  • To ensure hiring of BDE is completed in line with the budgeted headcount
  • b) Drive the team to Cross-sell multiple products to clients
Measures and Targets:
  • As per Performance scorecard.
  • Hiring as per the budgeted headcount
  • Cross-sell as per the allotted targets
Key Responsibilities

Human Capital / People Management

Description

  • Second Line – Develop a strong 2nd line. Drive optimum span of control.
  • Attrition – Control DSR voluntary attrition levels as per score card.
  • Performance Management -100% NBO Participation for FLIP

Measures and Targets:

  • Ensure attrition levels are within the required limits, as per the scorecard.
  • To Ensure 100% NBO Participation for FLIP
  • Positive feedback from team on internal surveys and open house sessions conducted, if any.
Regulatory & Business Conduct
  • Display exemplary conduct and live by the Group’s Values and Code of Conduct.
  • Take personal responsibility for embedding the highest standards of ethics, including regulatory and business conduct, across Standard Chartered Bank. This includes understanding and ensuring compliance with, in letter and spirit, all applicable laws, regulations, guidelines and the Group Code of Conduct.
  • Lead to achieve the outcomes set out in the Bank’s Conduct Principles.
  • Effectively and collaboratively identify, escalate, mitigate and resolve risk, conduct and compliance matters.

Skills and Experience
  • Graduate/ Post Graduate, consistent academic career
  • Extensive sales experience (5 to 7 years)
  • Sales focused and highly target oriented
  • Able to pick up new concepts quickly
  • Able and excited about going out to meet new customers
  • Competitive awareness & benchmarking
  • Excellent communication, interpersonal & relationship building skills
  • Banking knowledge of the product
  • Management Information Skills
  • Good Interpersonal Skills
  • Customer and Service Orientation
Role Specific Technical Competencies
  • Good Interpersonal Skills
  • Customer and Service Orientation
  • Banking knowledge
  • Management Information Skills
  • Competitive awareness & benchmarking
  • Excellent communication, interpersonal & relationship building skills
  • Able to pick up new concepts quickly
  • Able and excited about going out to meet new customers
  • Coordinate customer events for the cluster along with the product team
  • Aggressive Sales call plans to acquire large prospective customers through referrals.
About Standard Chartered

We're an international bank, nimble enough to act, big enough for impact. For more than 170 years, we've worked to make a positive difference for our clients, communities, and each other. We question the status quo, love a challenge and enjoy finding new opportunities to grow and do better than before. If you're looking for a career with purpose and you want to work for a bank making a difference, we want to hear from you. You can count on us to celebrate your unique talents and we can't wait to see the talents you can bring us.

Our purpose, to drive commerce and prosperity through our unique diversity, together with our brand promise, to be here for good are achieved by how we each live our valued behaviours. When you work with us, you'll see how we value difference and advocate inclusion.

Together we:

  • Do the right thing and are assertive, challenge one another, and live with integrity, while putting the client at the heart of what we do
  • Never settle, continuously striving to improve and innovate, keeping things simple and learning from doing well, and not so well
  • Are better together, we can be ourselves, be inclusive, see more good in others, and work collectively to build for the long term
What we offer

In line with our Fair Pay Charter, we offer a competitive salary and benefits to support your mental, physical, financial and social wellbeing.

  • Core bank funding for retirement savings, medical and life insurance, with flexible and voluntary benefits available in some locations.
  • Time-off including annual leave, parental/maternity (20 weeks), sabbatical (12 months maximum) and volunteering leave (3 days), along with minimum global standards for annual and public holiday, which is combined to 30 days minimum.
  • Flexible working options based around home and office locations, with flexible working patterns.
  • Proactive wellbeing support through Unmind, a market-leading digital wellbeing platform, development courses for resilience and other human skills, global Employee Assistance Programme, sick leave, mental health first-aiders and all sorts of self-help toolkits
  • A continuous learning culture to support your growth, with opportunities to reskill and upskill and access to physical, virtual and digital learning.
  • Being part of an inclusive and values driven organisation, one that embraces and celebrates our unique diversity, across our teams, business functions and geographies - everyone feels respected and can realise their full potential.

title: Area Sales Specialist (Business Finance Specialist) - Medical Loans

description.text:
ABOUT TIDE

At Tide we help SMEs save time (and money) in the running of their businesses by not only offering business accounts and related banking services, but also a comprehensive set of highly usable and connected administrative solutions from invoicing to accounting.

Tide is transforming the small business banking market with over 1.8 million members globally across the UK, India, Germany and France.

Using advanced technology, all solutions are designed with SMEs in mind. With quick onboarding, low fees and innovative features, we thrive on making data-driven decisions to serve our mission: to help SMEs save both time (and money) so they can get back to doing what they love.

Tide facts:

Tide is available for UK, Indian, German and French SMEs
Over 1.8 million members: 800,000 UK and 1,000,000 in India and growing rapidly
Over $300 million raised in funding
Over 2,500 Tideans globally - we're diversity champions!
We have offices in Central London, with a member support and technology centre in Sofia, Bulgaria, technology centres in Serbia, Romania, Lithuania and Hyderabad and offices in Gurugram and New Delhi, and in Berlin, Paris and Luxembourg.

ABOUT THE TEAM

The Member Accession Marketing Team (India) plays a critical role in driving Tide's growth in the Indian market by acquiring and onboarding new SME members. The team builds targeted marketing strategies, ensures smooth onboarding journeys, and equips members to make the most of Tide's financial and administrative tools. Through localized campaigns, strong stakeholder relationships, and data-led optimisation, the team ensures sustained member engagement and positions Tide as the preferred platform for Indian businesses.

ABOUT THE ROLE

We are seeking a Senior Area Sales Specialist to drive the growth of our Medical Loans offering. The primary responsibility of this role is to identify, develop, and manage strategic partnerships with healthcare providers, insurance companies, financial institutions, brokers, and other relevant stakeholders to expand and promote medical loan offerings. The Partnerships Manager will be focused on driving sales and revenue generation by offering medical loans, ensuring seamless integration with partners, and building long-term relationships to foster business growth.
This role requires a blend of business development, relationship management, and financial expertise. The ideal candidate will have a deep understanding of the healthcare & finance sector and experience in partnership development, especially within the medical loans space & Hospital.

Key Responsibilities:
Identify, onboard, and maintain strategic partnerships with hospitals, healthcare providers, insurance companies, brokers, financial advisors, and other key stakeholders to promote medical loan offerings and drive business growth.
Drive sales by generating leads through healthcare providers, DSAs, FOS teams, and financial institutions. Develop targeted strategies to convert leads into loans, ensuring a consistent and growing pipeline of clients.
Onboard large hospital chains, clinics, and healthcare institutions to Tide's platform, ensuring they are actively engaged in lead generation for medical loans. Work closely with these institutions to develop tailored solutions for their patients, driving lead conversion into sales.
Create new revenue streams by leveraging partnerships. Develop campaigns with DSAs, FOS, and calling teams to promote medical loan products, ensuring effective conversion and closing of deals.
Lead negotiations with partners, structure deals, and collaborate with legal teams to finalize agreements that align with business objectives and regulatory requirements.
Educate partners on medical loan products, ensuring they understand the features, benefits, and terms. Provide ongoing support to resolve issues and ensure smooth operations.
Ensure that all partnerships and medical loan offerings comply with relevant financial, healthcare, and data privacy regulations.
WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR
4 to 8 years of experience in partnership management, business development, or sales, preferably in the healthcare finance, fintech, or financial services industries.
Experience in managing relationships with hospitals, Health care insurance companies is highly desirable.
Strong track record in building and maintaining strategic partnerships. Excellent negotiation, relationship management, and communication skills.
A deep understanding of medical loan products, the healthcare finance sector, and market dynamics.
Proven experience in generating revenue through partnerships and lead generation initiatives. Strong business development focus with the ability to convert leads into revenue
A Bachelors degree in Finance, Business, Economics, or a related field is required. An MBA or relevant advanced degree is a plus.
Ability to work independently and collaboratively within a team. Comfortable managing multiple stakeholders and competing priorities.
A genuine passion for healthcare access and driving innovation in medical finance solutions for patients and healthcare providers.
Experience working with financial institutions, healthcare providers, DSAs, or fintech companies is a plus. Previous team management experience is advantageous.
Additional Requirements:
Travel may be required to meet with partners and clients.
Ability to work independently as well as part of a team.
Previous experience working with a bank, fintech, or digital bank will be a plus.
Previous team handling experience will be a plus.

WHAT YOU'LL GET IN RETURN:
Competitive salary
Self & Family Health Insurance
Term & Life Insurance
OPD Benefits
Mental wellbeing through Plumm
Learning & Development Budget
15 days of Privilege leaves
12 days of Casual leaves
12 days of Sick leaves
3 paid days off for volunteering or L&D activities

TIDE IS A PLACE FOR EVERYONE

At Tide, we believe that we can only succeed if we let our differences enrich our culture. Our Tideans come from a variety of backgrounds and experience levels. We consider everyone irrespective of their ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, family or parental status, national origin, veteran, neurodiversity or differently-abled status. We celebrate diversity in our workforce as a cornerstone of our success. Our commitment to a broad spectrum of ideas and backgrounds is what enables us to build products that resonate with our members' diverse needs and lives.

We are One Team and foster a transparent and inclusive environment, where everyone's voice is heard.

At Tide, we thrive on diversity, embracing various backgrounds and experiences. We welcome all individuals regardless of ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, or disability. Our inclusive culture is key to our success, helping us build products that meet our members' diverse needs. We are One Team, committed to transparency and ensuring everyone's voice is heard.

Disclaimer

It has come to our attention that individuals or agencies are falsely claiming to represent Tide and are reaching out to candidates regarding job opportunities. Please be aware that:
Tide does not charge any fees at any stage of the recruitment process.
All official Tide job opportunities are listed exclusively on our Careers Page and applications should be submitted through this channel.
Communication from Tide will only come from an official @tide.co email address.
Tide does not work with agencies or recruiters without prior formal engagement, and we do not authorize third parties to make job offers on our behalf.

If you are contacted by anyone misrepresenting Tide or requesting payment, please treat it as fraudulent and report it to us immediately at talent@tide.co
Your safety and trust are important to us, and we are committed to ensuring a fair and transparent recruitment process.

Tide leverages AI to enhance our hiring experience. You can read more about how we use AI in our recruitment process in our AI Policy.

Your personal data will be processed by Tide for recruitment purposes and in accordance with Tide's Recruitment Privacy Notice.

description.text:
About Us

With electric vehicles expected to be nearly 30% of new vehicle sales by 2025 and more than 50% by 2040, electric mobility is becoming a reality. ChargePoint (NYSE: CHPT) is at the center of this revolution, powering one of the world's leading EV charging networks and a comprehensive set of hardware, software and mobile solutions for every charging need across North America and Europe. We bring together drivers, businesses, automakers, policymakers, utilities and other stakeholders to make e-mobility a global reality.

Since our founding in 2007, ChargePoint has focused solely on making the transition to electric easy for businesses, fleets and drivers. ChargePoint offers a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity to create an all-electric future and a trillion-dollar market.

At ChargePoint, we foster a positive and productive work environment by committing to live our values of Be Courageous, Charge Together, Love our Customers, Operate with Openness, and Relentlessly Pursue Awesome. These values guide how we show up every day, align, and work together to build a brighter future for all of us.

Join the team that is building the EV charging industry and make your mark on how people and goods will get everywhere they need to go, in any context, for generations to come.

Reports To

NOC Delivery Manager

What You Will Be Doing
NOC Support Engineers will provide technical expertise in resolving and fixing the problems arising in the ChargePoint's array of Charging stations.
You have to define and diagnose resolutions for the issues and drive resolution of charging stations faults, and systematically reduce critical outages and improve overall uptime of the Charging Stations.
You will be analyzing, diagnosing, isolating and resolving problems and documenting your analysis in the case notes.
You will also work collaboratively with other services teams.
You learn and understand the various technological aspects of different models of the ChargePoint Charging stations and will handle the situation and drive towards faster resolution.
You will work in a ITIL service lifecycle Framework and ensure MTTR and MTBF are achieved.
You will Create technical documentation and make improvements to processes and procedures.
Perform proactive preventative maintenance for both hardware and software.
You will work in a Shift that spans 24 hours in various shift timings and you will work from the Office all 5 days of the week.
What You Will Bring to ChargePoint
Tenacious commitment to customer focused support
Comfort with making complex decisions and excited by "thinking outside the box"
Must have exceptional troubleshooting and advanced analytical thinking skills for complex issue resolution between hardware/software components
Ability to accurately convey complex concepts in easily digestible communications both verbally and in writing
Proven experience in driving initiatives, cross-functional collaborations, and holding others accountable to expectations
Account management skills including investigating and understanding current and historical issues for specific accounts
Maintains effectiveness when experiencing major changes in personal work tasks or work environment; adjusts effectively to work within new work structures, processes, requirements, or cultures
Ability to work effectively and complete tasks/projects with minimal supervision
Proactively identify areas of product improvement including design enhancements, failure analysis, and quality control
Focuses and guides self in accomplishing work objectives
Deals effectively with others in antagonistic situations, using appropriate interpersonal styles and methods to reduce tension or conflict
Actively appreciates and includes the diverse capabilities, insights, and ideas of others and working effectively and respectfully with individuals of diverse backgrounds, styles, abilities, and motivation
Sets high standards of performance for self; assuming responsibility and accountability for successfully completing assignments or tasks; self-imposing standards of excellence rather than having standards imposed
Requirements
Minimum of 6 years in a technical support environment for complex hardware and software solutions
Working experience, certifications, or bachelor's degree in technical field
The ideal candidate should be from any of the following experience - Network engineer - Systems engineer – Linux, Unix or Windows engineer - Backup or Storage engineer - Electrical or Electronics engineer - Technical Support engineer who has ability to understand and diagnose the technical aspect in any technological domains.
The ideal candidate should have previous exposure and working knowledge into CLI interface of more or more flavors of Linux (ex: RedHat, Ubuntu etc). The candidate can also have working experience in Unix.
The ideal candidate is well versed in Industry standards practices and solutions.
The candidate should have good exposure and understanding of the Operational processes of Incident Management, Change Management and Problem Management.
Understand the ITIL or any other operations Service lifecycle frameworks or Project management or quality frameworks.
Desirable to have candidates with well versed knowledge in Microsoft Word, Excel and Powerpoint.
Its desirable if the candidate has knowledge on Power distribution and/or power electronics fundamentals, familiarity with AC/DC EV charging protocols: CCS, CHAdeMO, J1772 etc.
The candidate should be an excellent communicator with skills in Documentation and creating knowledge articles.
Desirable to have worked in CRM tools like Salesforce, Servicenow, Jira etc.

Location

Bangalore, India

We are committed to an inclusive and diverse team. ChargePoint is an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate based on race, color, ethnicity, ancestry, national origin, religion, sex, gender, gender identity, gender expression, sexual orientation, age, disability, veteran status, genetic information, marital status or any legally protected status.

If there is a match between your experiences/skills and the Company needs, we will contact you directly.

ChargePoint is committed to fostering an inclusive workplace that welcomes and supports all qualified individuals. In alignment with this commitment, we ensure that persons with disabilities are provided with reasonable accommodations throughout the employment process.

If you need a reasonable accommodation to participate in the application or interview process, to perform essential job functions, or to access any other benefits and privileges of employment, please contact us at accommodations@chargepoint.com.

ChargePoint is an equal opportunity employer.
Applicants only - Recruiting agencies do not contact.

employer.name: Bayer
employer.briefDescription: Bayer is a global enterprise with core competencies in the life science fields of healthcare & agriculture. Explore how you can impact our mission: Health for all, Hunger for none at career.bayer.com.

description.text:
At Bayer we’re visionaries, driven to solve the world’s toughest challenges and striving for a world where ,Health for all, Hunger for none’ is no longer a dream, but a real possibility. We’re doing it with energy, curiosity and sheer dedication, always learning from unique perspectives of those around us, expanding our thinking, growing our capabilities and redefining ‘impossible’. There are so many reasons to join us. If you’re hungry to build a varied and meaningful career in a community of brilliant and diverse minds to make a real difference, there’s only one choice.

Warehouse Transport Last Mile Manager -

POSITION PURPOSE:

The Warehouse Transport Last Mile Manager – Consumer Health (CH) ensures the reliable, cost‑efficient, compliant and high-quality distribution of products across India. The primary focus is to drive exceptional performance in on-time, in-full product supply, while aggressively pursuing cost optimization strategies. The role will also focus on managing and optimizing relationships with local service providers across warehousing and transportation.

YOUR TASKS AND RESPONSIBILITIES:

Warehouse Management:

Compliance and Operations: Manage warehouse operations in alignment with company policies and vision, overseeing receiving, warehousing, distribution, and maintenance. Optimize layout for efficient space utilization.
Strategic Location Planning: Strategize warehouse locations across 29 states in relation to customer locations to enhance service delivery.
Cost Optimization: Identify and implement significant cost-saving measures.
Stakeholder Engagement: Liaise with large external Clearing and Forwarding Agents (CFAs) and transport entities, building relationships with senior stakeholders.
Collaboration for Stock Movement: Work closely with the supply chain (planning, supply coordinator and customer solutions) and EXIM Customs to ensure efficient stock movement from CMOs (Contract Manufacturing Operations) to Central Warehouses (CWH) / HUB, and between CFAs based on stock movement needs.
Stakeholder Management: Actively engage with stakeholders to manage ad-hoc processes at CFAs, warehouses, and transport operations, including scrapping activities.
Inventory Management: Oversee stock segregation (saleable, expired, leakage, breakages), and conduct physical verification and reconciliation of inventory on a monthly, quarterly, and annual basis.
Stock Handling: Ensure smooth handling of stocks at CFAs and timely dispatch through approved transporters.
Regulatory Compliance: Verify that CWHs and CFAs possess valid storage and distribution licenses, comply with local FDA regulations, and have Goods and Service Tax registrations for repacking activities.
Digitization and Improvement: Drive digitization initiatives within the warehouse network and pursue operational excellence by identifying continuous improvement opportunities in collaboration with global process owners.
Policy Development and Training: Collaborate with local, regional, and global stakeholders to develop country commercial policies and train the service delivery network on operational procedures.
Audit Support: Ensure expired stocks with approved write-offs are sent for incineration, destruction, or scrapping, and support audits by addressing queries promptly.
Cost Monitoring: Actively monitor warehouse-related costs against budget, identify deviation drivers, and implement continuous improvement activities.
Compliance Assurance: Safeguard compliance and ensure adherence to internal and external regulations, including a yearly evaluation of CFA operations performance.

Transportation Management:

Transporter Collaboration: Work with Procurement and Country Quality teams to appoint transporters and ensure contracts are in place.
Transporter Performance Evaluation: Conduct yearly evaluations of transporter performance.
Shipment Planning: Plan and supervise shipments from production to end-users, including multi-drop scheduling for daily and weekly routes.
Material Tracking: Monitor the movement of materials using functional systems.
Cost Management: Monitor transportation costs and recommend cost-effective shipping methods.
Logistics Support: Provide seamless logistics support to enhance customer service for commercial stakeholders.
Temperature Monitoring: Timely share temperature readings of vehicles post-delivery with the Country Quality team.
Cost Monitoring: Collaborate with the country finance business partner to track EOF-related costs against the budget and drive continuous improvement activities.
Logistical Optimization: Ensure cost benefits to the bottom line by optimizing logistical activities while staying updated on safety regulations.
Climate Change Initiatives: Address climate change issues by implementing transport strategies and monitoring the organization’s carbon footprint.
Inventory Management: Oversee inventory at the Central Warehouse/HUB and billing locations, coordinating with internal stakeholders and Contract Manufacturing Managers to ensure stock availability and prevent sales loss.
Product Segregation: Ensure products are segregated by division and legal entity, with saleable, expired, and near-expiry items stored separately.
Return Coordination: Work with CFAs and distributors on stock returns upon delivery, investigating reasons for returns.
Verification of Returns: Ensure that returned stock from customers is physically verified and timely orders are processed to avoid blocks.
Warehouse Selection: Ensure selection of warehouses and CFAs complies with guidelines.
Customer Complaint Resolution: Address customer complaints regarding dispatches within service level agreements.
Audit Observations: Ensure timely closure of audit observations and streamline processes as necessary.
Logistics Budget Management: Review and monitor the optimal utilization of the logistics budget.
Safety and Regulatory Compliance: Adhere to all standard processes for safety, health, environment, and applicable statutory regulations.
Warehouse and Transportation Management: Efficiently manage warehouses and transportation for customer dispatches and returns as per guidelines.
Agreement Renewals: Monitor and ensure timely renewal of all logistics-related agreements.
CFA Management: Oversee CFAs across the country, along with one Central Warehouse and one destruction hub.
CFA Audits: Conduct visits to CFAs for audits and to monitor overall function.
Inventory Reconciliation: Manage inventory and reconcile physical stocks on a monthly, quarterly, and annual basis using SAP.
Commission Verification: Verify commission calculations for CFAs based on sales generated.
Transporter Coordination: Finalize transporters and couriers at all locations regarding freight rates, payments, and performance.
Regulatory Liaison: Coordinate with local regulatory functions for the renewal of drug licenses as required.
Audit Support: Assist internal and external audits with necessary documentation and explanations in consultation with CFAs.
Tax Liaison: Work with the tax function on GST topics, e-way bills, e-invoicing, and compliance with government notifications.
Sample Coordination: Manage redress for imported finished goods samples at local manufacturing sites and coordinate with CMOs for sample conversion.
Finance Coordination: Provide inventory details for all billing locations, including manufacturing sites, to cover inventory insurance.
Write-Off Management: Prepare documentation for write-offs of expired, damaged, or leaked goods, obtain stakeholder approvals, and enter write-off entries into the system.
Trade Association Liaison: Liaise with trade associations regarding sales stoppages or other issues.

WHO YOU ARE:

Bachelor degree of Commerce preferably or post-graduation /diploma in Material Management or qualification in supply chain management will be preferred.
 5 – 10 years of post-qualification experience gained at a leading multi-national Company and having deep understanding of Warehouse
Management and Logistics operations with specific focus on managing temperature control goods.
Hands on experience in working on SAP (SD & MM Module)
Digital mind set and knowledge of common supply chain systems and emerging technology.
Ability to use judgment to provide solutions to complex business problems and think beyond local context.
Strong stakeholder management capabilities and comfortable working with all levels of management.
Good knowledge of accounting practices.
Mature business ethics and deep understanding of applicable regulatory requirements.
Extensive knowledge of process improvement and change management approaches.
Assertive, competent, confident and resilient.
Capable to handle stressful situations.
Excellent analytical and communication skills.
Well-developed English language skills, both written and oral.
Ability to embrace change and be able to work in a changing environment.

YOUR APPLICATION

Bayer is an equal opportunity employer that strongly values fairness and respect at work. We welcome applications from all individuals, regardless of race, religion, gender, age, physical characteristics, disability, sexual orientation etc. We are committed to treating all applicants fairly and avoiding discrimination.

Location: India : Maharashtra : Thane
Division: Pharmaceuticals
Reference Code: 866752

Contact Us

+ 022-25311234
description.html:
At Bayer we’re visionaries, driven to solve the world’s toughest challenges and striving for a world where ,Health for all, Hunger for none’ is no longer a dream, but a real possibility. We’re doing it with energy, curiosity and sheer dedication, always learning from unique perspectives of those around us, expanding our thinking, growing our capabilities and redefining ‘impossible’. There are so many reasons to join us. If you’re hungry to build a varied and meaningful career in a community of brilliant and diverse minds to make a real difference, there’s only one choice.

Warehouse Transport Last Mile Manager -

POSITION PURPOSE:
The Warehouse Transport Last Mile Manager – Consumer Health (CH) ensures the reliable, cost‑efficient, compliant and high-quality distribution of products across India. The primary focus is to drive exceptional performance in on-time, in-full product supply, while aggressively pursuing cost optimization strategies. employer.name: Solera
employer.briefDescription: Solera is the global leader in vehicle lifecycle management software-as-a-service (SaaS), data, and services through vehicle claims, vehicle repairs, vehicle solutions, and fleet solutions.

description.text:
Software Engineer (P2)

Solera is a global leader in data and software services that strives to transform every touchpoint of the vehicle lifecycle into a connected digital experience. In addition, we provide products and services to protect life’s other most important assets: our homes and digital identities. Today, Solera processes over 300 million digital transactions annually for approximately 235,000 partners and customers in more than 90 countries. Our 6,500 team members foster an uncommon, innovative culture and are dedicated to successfully bringing the future to bear today through cognitive answers, insights, algorithms and automation. For more information, please visit solera.com.

The Role

We are looking for a Software Engineer (.NET) with 3–5 years of experience to join our dynamic engineering team. The ideal candidate should have strong hands-on experience in .NET technologies, SQL Server, cloud platforms (AWS/Azure), and front-end frameworks.

You will contribute to designing, developing, and maintaining scalable applications while working closely with cross-functional teams in an agile environment.

What You’ll Do

Develop and maintain web applications using .NET Core / ASP.NET MVC, C#, and SQL Server
Build and consume RESTful APIs and microservices
Work on both backend and frontend components using React or Angular, HTML, CSS, JavaScript
Implement and follow design patterns and clean architecture principles
Debug, troubleshoot, and optimize application performance
Participate in code reviews, unit testing, and CI/CD processes
Work with cloud platforms such as AWS and/or Azure for deployment and monitoring
Contribute to AI-enabled features, including integration of Generative AI / LLM-based services via APIs
Write optimized SQL queries and ensure database performance tuning
Collaborate with product managers and senior engineers to understand requirements and deliver quality solutions

What You’ll Bring

Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field (or equivalent experience).
3–5 years of experience in software development
Strong experience in:
C#
ASP.NET / .NET Core
MVC architecture
SQL Server (queries, stored procedures, performance tuning)
Experience with React or Angular
Good understanding of HTML, CSS, JavaScript
Experience with AWS or Azure cloud services
Strong debugging and problem-solving skills
Understanding of Design Patterns and Object-Oriented Programming (OOP)
Experience integrating Generative AI / LLM services is a plus
Familiarity with Agile/Scrum methodology

Tech Stack:

Back-end: C#, .NET Framework 4.8, .Net Core 7+
Frontend: react or Angular
Data: SQL Server, MySQL, PostgreSQL; MongoDB; Elasticsearch/OpenSearch
AI: Power BI, LLM, MCP, RAG, AI Agents
Cloud/Infra: AWS (Lambda, ECS/EKS, API Gateway, S3, SQS/Event Bridge, RDS, MongoDB), Docker, Kubernetes, Terraform/CloudFormation, GitHub Actions/Jenkins

title: Protection Data Management, Analysis & Reporting Support

employer.name: United Nations
employer.briefDescription: United Nations - Shaping our future together The work of the UN reaches every corner of the globe, through actions on peacekeeping, human rights, humanitarian assistance, sustainable development...
employer.address:
1st Avenue at 46th Street
New York, NY 10017

description.text:
Work Location
Hybrid (Remote or Amman )
Expected duration
3-6 months
Duties and Responsibilities
The objective of this internship is to support the GFO Protection team, under the technical oversight of HQ, in improving the quality, accessibility, and usability of existing datasets, and to contribute to data analysis and reporting that informs both field-level and organizational decision-making. Scope of Work: Under the supervision of the Protection Analyst, and in coordination with HQ technical units, the intern will: • Support data entry and consolidation from various sources (ActivityInfo, Excel, reports, etc.) • Clean datasets by identifying and correcting errors, duplicates, and inconsistencies • Organize and maintain structured databases and filing systems • Assist in developing simple data management tools/templates (e.g., spreadsheets, trackers) • Conduct basic data analysis (descriptive statistics, summaries, simple visualizations) • Draft and contribute to regular reporting products in English (e.g., situation updates, summaries, briefing notes) • Support preparation of data summaries, dashboards, and presentations as needed • Ensure datasets and outputs are aligned with HQ data standards, tools, and reporting requirements • Support consolidation of data inputs for HQ-level analysis, reporting, and global information products • Liaise with relevant HQ focal points, as required, to ensure consistency in approaches and outputs • Ensure proper documentation of data processes and changes made • Maintain confidentiality and data protection standards at all times
Qualifications/special skills
• Currently enrolled in or recently graduated from a degree in Data Science, Statistics, Humanitarian Action, Economics, Social Sciences, Communications, or related field • Strong proficiency in Microsoft Excel (required) • Familiarity with data cleaning and basic analysis techniques • Knowledge of ActivityInfo and Power BI is an asset • Ability to handle large datasets accurately and efficiently • Ability to synthesize data into meaningful insights and narratives
Languages
• Strong written communication skills in English, with the ability to draft clear and concise reports
Additional Information
Not available.
Intern Specific text
Interns are not financially remunerated by the United Nations. Costs and arrangements for travel, visas, accommodation and living expenses are the responsibility of interns or their sponsoring institutions. Interns who are not citizens or permanent residents of the country where the internship is undertaken, may be required to obtain the appropriate visa and work/employment authorization. Successful candidates should discuss their specific visa requirements before accepting the internship offer.
No Fee
THE UNITED NATIONS DOES NOT CHARGE A FEE AT ANY STAGE OF THE RECRUITMENT PROCESS (APPLICATION, INTERVIEW MEETING, PROCESSING, OR TRAINING). THE UNITED NATIONS DOES NOT CONCERN ITSELF WITH INFORMATION ON APPLICANTS’ BANK ACCOUNTS.

employer.name: Chegg
employer.briefDescription: Chegg, the student hub, is transforming the way millions of students learn by connecting them to the people and tools needed to succeed thro

description.text:
Job Description

Senior Platform Engineer
Location: Remote

As Chegg Skills engineers, our software empowers students to move from “learning” to “earning”. We build the apps and systems that help students make life-changing career changes into high-growth job opportunities. We pride ourselves on our ability to provide accessible education to motivated career switchers. Together we build the tech that helps them reach mastery and success.

In this role, you will play a leading role in a fully remote team of diverse, motivated engineers that is changing what education looks like. We serve an audience of thousands of students and educators every year. Your contributions will shape the way these students learn, helping them succeed in life.

Chegg Skills is a new and growing division of Chegg focusing on post-collegiate demographics. We are growing the engineering team to help support our continued business expansion as more students take the next step in their careers with Chegg. Join us and help scale engineering to power our next phase of growth!

We expect that most qualified candidates will have at least 6 years of professional software development experience building user-facing web applications.

Within 1 month, you will:

Work with an onboarding buddy to push commits in your first week.
Use our documentation to learn the microservices architecture.
Start familiarizing yourself with our React.js, Next.js, and Node.js codebases and begin contributing.
Use code reviews and pairing with peers to raise code quality.
Deploy changes confidently with solid test coverage through our robust CI/CD pipeline.
Participate in sprint planning and retrospective meetings.
Get to know your remote peers and stakeholders over video calls.
Identify pain points in the team’s process and codebases with fresh eyes, and share ideas from past experiences.

Within 3 months, you will:

Collaborate with developers, product managers, and stakeholders to identify product opportunities.
Demonstrate expertise in using JS/React to quickly iterate toward product goals.
Employ industry best practices in your work, and share those practices with your peers.
Work across the stack and multiple applications to create user experiences that support our students’ learning.
Standardize our application data access by contributing to a new GraphQL layer.
Improve observability across the system through standardized logging, metrics, and dashboards.
Maximize your impact in an environment that strives for high focus and infrequent engineering interruptions.
Invest in a culture of accountability by holding yourself and your peers to high standards.
Receive regular performance feedback from your manager and peers.

Within 6 months, you will:

Influence the roadmap and product development of Chegg Skills systems.
Expand your knowledge and influence by rotating across project pods and working in new codebases and product areas.
Lead research and architecture initiatives to support Chegg Skills’ rapid growth.
Hone your skills with accessibility training and make accessibility part of your “definition of done”.
Standardize and document operating procedures.
Invest in junior developers through mentoring relationships.
Develop a career growth plan and goals with your manager.

Why do we exist?

Students are working harder than ever before to stabilize their future. Our recent research study called State of the Student shows that nearly 3 out of 4 students are working to support themselves through college and 1 in 3 students feel pressure to spend more than they can afford. We founded our business on provided affordable textbook rental options to address these issues. Since then, we’ve expanded our offerings to supplement many facets of higher educational learning through Chegg Study, Chegg Math, Chegg Writing, Chegg Internships, Chegg Skills, and more to support students beyond their college experience. These offerings lower financial concerns for students by modernizing their learning experience. We exist so students everywhere have a smarter, faster, more affordable way to student.
About Us
What is Chegg?

An ‘always on’ digital learning platform.

Chegg puts students first…Everything we build in this company is student-focused, making us the leading student-first connected learning platform. Chegg strives to improve the overall return on investment in education by helping students learn more in less time and at a lower cost. This is achieved by providing students a multitude of educational tools from affordable textbook rentals to Chegg Study which supplements their learning through 24/7 tutor access, step-by-step help with questions, and more.

Chegg is a publicly-held company based in Santa Clara, California and trades on the NYSE under the symbol CHGG.

  • Develop a career growth plan and goals with your manager.


Why do we exist?

Students are working harder than ever before to stabilize their future. Our recent research study called State of the Student shows that nearly 3 out of 4 students are working to support themselves through college and 1 in 3 students feel pressure to spend more than they can afford. We founded our business on provided affordable textbook rental options to address these issues. Since then, we’ve expanded our offerings to supplement many facets of higher educational learning through Chegg Study, Chegg Math, Chegg Writing, Chegg Internships, Chegg Skills, and more to support students beyond their college experience. These offerings lower financial concerns for students by modernizing their learning experience. We exist so students everywhere have a smarter, faster, more affordable way to student.


About Us

What is Chegg?


An ‘always on’ digital learning platform.


Chegg puts students first…Everything we build in this company is student-focused, making us the leading student-first connected learning platform. Chegg strives to improve the overall return on investment in education by helping students learn more in less time and at a lower cost. This is achieved by providing students a multitude of educational tools from affordable textbook rentals to Chegg Study which supplements their learning through 24/7 tutor access, step-by-step help with questions, and more.


Chegg is a publicly-held company based in Santa Clara, California and trades on the NYSE under the symbol CHGG.

employer.name: OpenText
employer.briefDescription: A global leader in Enterprise Information Management solutions, we enable intelligent & connected enterprises and inspire new ways to work.
employer.ceoName: James McGourlay, Interim Chief Executive Officer

description.text:
AI-First. Future-Driven. Human-Centered.
At OpenText, AI is at the heart of everything we do—powering innovation, transforming work, and empowering digital knowledge workers. We're hiring talent that AI can't replace to help us shape the future of information management. Join us.

Your Impact:
The Lead Product Manager will be responsible for shaping and executing the product strategy for OpenText’s core B2B communications platform.This role focuses on defining strategy, driving roadmap execution, and ensuring the platform delivers measurable business and operational outcomes. The Lead PM will work closely with Engineering, Architecture, and Operations to evolve the platform over multiple horizons, while acting as a senior product voice for communications capabilities across the integration ecosystem.
What the role offers:

• Own the product strategy, and multi quarter roadmap for the communications platform
• Ensure the platform supports any to any connectivity across heterogeneous partner environments, protocols, and deployment models
• Frame product decisions around business and operational outcomes (e.g., scalability, resilience, reliability, time to connect, efficiency)
• Drive prioritization and trade-off decisions in partnership with Engineering and Architecture, balancing customer needs, technical constraints, and operational impact
• Guide the introduction of automation and intelligent capabilities to reduce manual effort and operational overhead
• Partner with Engineering and Operations to establish and monitor measurable service level objectivesInfluence architectural standards and long term platform investments
• Act as a subject matter expert and product authority for communications capabilities within the broader integration portfolioCommunicate clearly with executive stakeholders on priorities, trade-offs, and risks

What you need to succeed:

• 5+ years of experience in product management, with 10+ years of total professional experience
• Strong experience working on platform, infrastructure, or enterprise-scale systems rather than standalone end-user features
• Strong systems thinking across reliability, scalability, security, and platform abstraction
• Demonstrated ability to partner deeply with engineers, architects, and operations teams to drive outcomes
• Comfortable influencing decisions and alignment without formal people-management responsibilityPreferred
• Experience with enterprise connectivity, integration, or network centric platforms
• Familiarity with multiple B2B interaction models (e.g., synchronous, asynchronous, event-driven, batch, API-based)
• Exposure to automation, intelligence, or analytics applied to large-scale operational platforms

One last thing:
We are seeking a Lead Product Manager to lead the strategy, roadmap, and evolution of this foundational communications platform.
OpenText Business Network enables intelligent, connected, and trusted B2B interactions at global scale. A core part of this capability is our communications platform, which underpins how enterprises and their trading partners connect, exchange information, and operate reliably across complex ecosystems.

OpenText's efforts to build an inclusive work environment go beyond simply complying with applicable laws. Our Employment Equity and Diversity Policy provides direction on maintaining a working environment that is inclusive of everyone, regardless of culture, national origin, race, color, gender, gender identification, sexual orientation, family status, age, veteran status, disability, religion, or other basis protected by applicable laws.
If you need assistance and/or a reasonable accommodation due to a disability during the application or recruiting process, please submit a ticket atAsk HR. Our proactive approach fosters collaboration, innovation, and personal growth, enriching OpenText's vibrant workplace.

description.text:
OPENTEXT - THE INFORMATION COMPANY
OpenText is a global leader in information management, where innovation, creativity, and collaboration are the key components of our corporate culture. As a member of our team, you will have the opportunity to partner with the most highly regarded companies in the world, tackle complex issues, and contribute to projects that shape the future of digital transformation.

AI-First. Future-Driven. Human-Centered.
At OpenText, AI is at the heart of everything we do—powering innovation, transforming work, and empowering digital knowledge workers. We're hiring talent that AI can't replace to help us shape the future of information management. Join us.

Your Impact:
OpenText enables the digital world as the global leader in Enterprise Information Management, both on-cloud and off-cloud. We embrace all things digital and are committed to being the Best Place to Work for our Employees in over 140 locations around the world.
The Open Text Professional Services team is comprised of more than 3,000 consultants who function as trusted advisors, managing long-lasting relationships with more than 100,000 customers around the world. They are responsible for the delivery of business solutions from pre-sales to post implementation. Our teams of experts help organizations solve issues, create value, improve business performance, and work more effectively in an increasingly digital world.
As a Senior Consultant at OpenText Professional Services team you will play a key role in creating and delivering strategic, impactful, and integrated digital solutions for clients using a combination of OpenText technologies. This role offers an opportunity to leverage your expertise in SAP ABAP, OpenText VIM configuration, basic knowledge of SAP business processes and integration to deliver seamless solutions that bridge the world of information and business application.
What the role offers:

• Engage with enterprise architects, business, and IT stakeholders to understand business processes, technology landscape, and requirements, and define integrated digital solutions.
• Plan and conduct business and technical workshops to elicit, analyze, and document complex functional and technical requirements.
• Analyze customer requirements, evaluate solution options, and design robust, scalable, and foolproof architecture and solution designs.
• Create comprehensive solution documentation across the SDLC, including business requirements, solution blueprints, high- and low-level designs, and deployment/configuration guides.
• Collaborate closely with project managers, architects, and client teams to support planning, implementation, acceptance, and rollout of solutions.
• Configure and integrate OpenText VIM and related components with SAP and OpenText solutions (BCC, IC4S, Archive Center, Enterprise Scan, and Document Pipeline); assess change impacts and support releases and migrations.
• Troubleshoot and resolve complex functional and technical issues, perform root cause analysis, and mentor junior team members with technical and professional guidance.

What you need to succeed:

• Graduate/Postgraduate with 9-12 years of professional experience in designing medium to complex digital enterprise solutions in the areas OpenText products like VIM, BCC, IC4S, Archive Center.
• Excellent consulting skills – engagement, articulation, elicitation and influencing.
• Excellent customer-facing skills - stakeholder management, relationship building and negotiation – with strategic focus.
• Software configuration and programming knowledge, e.g., ABAP Programming, Data Dictionary, OOABAP, Fiori& Development RICEF objects.
• Basic knowledge in SAP MM (handling PO, GR, SES etc.) and FI modules with Invoice posting/blocking/parking scenarios.
• Experience in OpenText VIM installations, upgrades& Patching, Migrations.
• Excellent verbal and written communication and presentations skills.
• Willingness to travel within India and globally.

One last thing:
OpenText is more than just a corporation, it's a global community where trust is foundational, the bar is raised, and outcomes are owned.Join us on our mission to drive positive change through privacy, technology, and collaboration. At OpenText, we don't just have a culture; we have character. Choose us because you want to be part of a company that embraces innovation and empowers its employees to make a difference.

OpenText's efforts to build an inclusive work environment go beyond simply complying with applicable laws. Our Employment Equity and Diversity Policy provides direction on maintaining a working environment that is inclusive of everyone, regardless of culture, national origin, race, color, gender, gender identification, sexual orientation, family status, age, veteran status, disability, religion, or other basis protected by applicable laws.
If you need assistance and/or a reasonable accommodation due to a disability during the application or recruiting process, please contact us athr@opentext.com. Our proactive approach fosters collaboration, innovation, and personal growth, enriching OpenText's vibrant workplace.

title: Executive/ Sr. Executive Human Resource (Talent Acquisition)

employer.name: Rentokil Initial
employer.briefDescription: Rentokil Initial is a global business services company, operating in over 80 countries worldwide and offering outstanding customer service.
employer.address: Crawley, West Sussex, South East England, England

occupations.MGTWW: Business Management, Administrative & Customer Support Occupations

description.text:
About the Role:

 The Executive/Sr.Executive/Assistant Manager will be responsible for strategizing, sourcing, contacting, screening and assisting in the placement of qualified candidates as per the business requirements. The incumbent will be acting as a strong business and consulting partner where he/she will partner with the hiring manager for all business lines.

 The person will report to Lead - Talent Acquisition. The person will have to work as part of a multi-functional team and this involves working with the internal and external stakeholders across India.

Job Responsibilities:

 Understand recruitment requirements and plan strategies considering role dynamics and TAT, managing the end-to-end recruitment cycle.

 Collaborate with hiring managers to define job requirements and assess candidates against set criteria.

 Handle volume hiring for frontline sales and service roles within defined TAT and monthly targets.

 Source, screen, and interview candidates using channels like referrals, job portals, headhunting, and databases.

 Manage salary negotiations and the offer generation process.

 Maintain recruitment MIS/reports for management review.

 Build and manage a talent pipeline to ensure a positive applicant experience aligned with the employer brand.

 Use tools like ATS, HRMS, and manage the company’s career and referral portals.

 Coordinate with internal teams for inductions, data management, and reporting.

 Support technician hiring, onboarding, and related MIS updates.

 Handle ad-hoc recruitment tasks as per business needs.

Key Result Areas:

 Timely closure of different positions as per TAT

 Analysing recruitment cost for own portfolio

 Positions closed each Month

 Timely submission & 0 errors in candidate documentation

 Timely updation & 0 errors in reports

 Referral Scheme Awareness

Competencies (Skills essential to the role):

 Excellent Communication and Interpersonal skills

 Ability to multi-task and prioritise work

 Result oriented

 Excellent Negotiation skills

 Networking skills

 Well-focused and confident team player

 Proactive and Solution driven

 Business & Industry knowledge

 Detail oriented

Requirements:

Educational Qualification / Other Requirement:

 Any graduate with a minimum of 1 -3 years of recruiting experience of which a minimum of 1 year should be in front-line sales & service staff hiring

 Should have a basic knowledge of MS Office/G-Suite

 Should be willing to travel on a need basis

Benefits:

What can you expect from RPCI?

➔ Our values lie at the core of our mission and vision. We believe that it’s our people who make our company what it is. We believe in:

 Service

 Relationships

 Teamwork

 Responsibility

DEI statement: At RPCI, we believe in commitment to build an inclusive, varied workplace welcoming to people of all backgrounds.

title: Manager - Business Intelligence and Insights Group

description.text:
About Axis Max Life

Axis Max Life Insurance Limited, formerly known as Max Life Insurance Company Ltd., is a joint venture between Max Financial Services Limited (“MFSL”) and Axis Bank Limited.

Axis Max Life Insurance offers comprehensive protection and long-term savings life insurance solutions through its multi-channel distribution, including agency and third-party distribution partners. It has built its operations over two decades through a need-based sales process, a customer-centric approach to engagement and service delivery, and a well-trained human capital.

Axis Max Life has been consistently ranked among the best workplaces by the GPTW Institute, reflecting its commitment to creating a positive and empowering work environment.

#ComeAsYouAre LGBTQIA+ and PwD candidates of all ages are encouraged to apply

Job Description

Axis Max Life Insurance

Established in year 2000, Axis Max Life Insurance is currently 4th largest private life insurer and the largest non-bank life insurer in India. It is subsidiary of the publicly listed Max Financial Services Limited and joint venture between Max India Ltd and Axis Bank. The Company has more than 40 Lakh customers, with gross written premium of Rs. 19,018 crores (FY21), shareholders profit after tax at Rs. 523 crores and solvency ratio of 202%, 1.35 times more than the mandatory 150% indicating the company’s strong and stable financial position. Max Life has paid 99.35% claims on individual business policies and 99.27% claims on group business policies. It currently offers wide portfolio of life insurance products - Protection insurance, health, pension, annuity, child, retirement, savings and growth plans. Max Life has 10000+ employees, with presence in presence in 140+ cities across India and is ranked 18th Great Places to Work in India (the only Life insurance company in top 50).

Business Intelligence & Insights Group (BIIG)

BIIG is the centralized business intelligence and analytics team and is tasked with providing critical information and insights needed to manage the business and drive data driven decisions. BIIG team is working on building a data culture, enabling data democratization, reducing information overload and equipping everyone with information / insights needed to excel in their roles.

We are looking for a passionate leader to lead entire reporting for all distribution functions. You need to have a strong desire to lead change, drive efficiency, challenge the status quo and rationalize information overload. In this role, you will work closely with distribution leaders to understand key metrics, information needs and develop business intelligence products to cater to those needs. You will also define the strategy and roadmap for the team to accelerate our journey of becoming a data driven organization.

Job Description

Position

Manager- MIS Reporting

Incumbent

Department BIIG Function BIIG
Reporting to

SM/CM – BIIG

 Band 5B
Location 90 C Gurgaon – Head Office

Team size (D/I)

 D-Direct

I-Indirect

1-2 Direct reports

Total size- 3+ (incld. partners)

 JOB SUMMARY

· Define the vision and roadmap for business intelligence team and champion data culture within Axis Max Life.

· Lead and enable transformation to embrace automation and providing concise & real-time insights.

· Responsible for delivery of accurate and timely reports / dashboards.

· Coach and mentor the team to continuously improve skills and capabilities.

· Lead a team of 5+ professionals, including partners.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

· Handle Distribution reporting requirements across functions and job families for enabling strategic priorities and performance management. Responsibilities also include supporting CXO reports and dashboards

· Ensure timely & accurate delivery of reporting across functions and roles.

· Proactively identify opportunities to automate reporting requirements and build delivery capabilities on digital information assets in liaison with technology function. Integrate analytics with reporting assets.

· Drive data democratization culture by designing data assets.

· Collaborate to design and build data products for Distribution teams.

· Partner with data team to build data infrastructure necessary to facilitate efficient analysis, reporting and is relevant with the needs of the organization.

· Develop, coach and mentor team to become best in class business intelligence team.

Education: Master’s degree (M. Sc., M. Tech., MBA or any quantitative field)

Experience:

· Financial services exposure is desirable and preferred.

· Python & PowerBI

· At least 6-7 years of relevant experience in working in business reporting teams.

Key competencies/skills required

· Demonstrated experience of working with senior leadership / management.

· Experience in standardizing, streamlining and automating business reporting.

· Technical proficiency in BI tech stack –SQL Server reporting services, SAP BO, Python & Advanced Excel etc.

· Analyzing Complex data & Project Management Skills

· Have experience with open source BI tools.

· Well versed with data architecture / data warehousing / data lakes.

· Domain understanding of BFSI industry.

· Excellent interpersonal skills, strong verbal and written communication skills.

State

Home Office

Branch

Gurugram -90C

Department

Business Intelligence and Insights Group

Function

BIIG - Distribution

Posted On

04-May-2026

title: Data Management Strategy & Governance Senior Analyst

employer.name: The Cigna Group
employer.briefDescription: The Cigna Group is a global health service company, dedicated to helping the people we serve improve their health, well-being and sense of security.
employer.address:
900 Cottage Grove Rd.
Bloomfield CT, United States 06002

description.text:
About the Team and Role:

• Cigna IH consolidates data into aCommonDataModel andIntegratedDataStore to enable delivery of a seamless Digital experience to all of our stakeholders, clients, plan members, healthcare providers …
• The Data Mapping team within Cigna IH provides mapping services and expertise to any team or project within Cigna IH that needs consolidated data.
• Your role will be a hands-on role, responsible for delivering these mapping services to our stakeholders.
• You will collaborate with team members, data analysts, systems analysts, product and business partners to evaluate and validate requirements and do the most optimal data mapping against our common data model. You will also be participating in further developing our Data Management Tooling platform.

Skills/Tasks

• Delivery of the Data Mapping team you will work in, work with ecosystem, product managers, scrum masters and product owners to ensure that work is completed and that queries, issues, escalations are resolved.
• Owning individual mappings for inbound or outbound mapping, own updates to mapping documentation, monitor evolving scope perform gap analysis, plan and lead workshops, follow-up on CRs to incorporate in plan, manage SMEs and allocate pre-work and research, provide metrics, action data QA feedback.
• Build SQL queries to check the results from the mapping workshops.
• Validating change requests with full team, perform impact analysis, update and Cigna Data Model
• Establishing and maintain strong business relationships with key stakeholders.

About you:

• Problem solver with excellent analytical skills and people skills
• Strong communication and presentation skills with ability to organize and facilitate workshops with several stakeholders
• Should be resourceful, work well under pressure, and able to keep up in a fast-paced international environment.
• Extremely detail oriented, with excellent organization skills
• Ability to build strong working relationships across the organization
• Strong preference for Data Management ; able to bridge Data Engineering teams (Mapping, Build Test data flows, Data Governance) and business/project teams.
• Capable of writing SQL code to check the results from the workshops. As we operate in an Agile environment, as a full-stack team member you might assist in building and maintaining SQL (and SSIS) code to actually ingest the mapped data into the Integrated Data Store.
• Have experience with Cloud based technology (AWS)
• Understanding of Healthcare Insurance industry

Ideally you will have experience with:

• Extensive experience of data analytics / business analytics experience strongly preferred
• Experience with standard tools such as MS Excel
• Experience with SQL and affinity with Relational Data Models
• Proven ability to manage stakeholder communications and facilitate workshops, demonstrating strong verbal and written skills
• Demonstrated knowledge of healthcare products, benefits, claims, and/or customer service or related experience
• Experience with Data Management tool Collibra (or comparable) is a plus
• Experience with implementing AI tools for Source-to-Target Mapping

About The Cigna Group

Cigna Healthcare, a division of The Cigna Group, is an advocate for better health through every stage of life. We guide our customers through the health care system, empowering them with the information and insight they need to make the best choices for improving their health and vitality. Join us in driving growth and improving lives.

description.text:
Job Description
We are seeking an experienced and client-focused Product Owner with 6–8 years of experience to drive product vision, strategy and delivery of service-based Projects. The ideal candidate will have a strong background in requirement gathering, stakeholder management, and pre-sales support, along with proven experience in leading BA efforts across projects ensuring that product goals are clearly defined, prioritized, and delivered with high value to end users.
This role requires working closely with US-based clients, delivery teams, and sales stakeholders to translate business needs into scalable technology solutions. The candidate should be comfortable operating in both delivery and pre-sales environments, contributing to business growth as well as successful project execution.

Key Responsibilities
Own and manage the product backlog, defining and prioritizing features, user stories, and tasks based on business value.
Lead end-to-end requirement gathering, analysis, and document project Roadmap.
Collaborate with clients to understand business goals and translate them into functional and technical requirements.
Drive pre-sales activities, including solutioning, proposal creation (RFP/RFI responses), effort estimation, and client presentations.
Act as a bridge between business stakeholders and technical teams, ensuring clarity and alignment throughout the project lifecycle.
Facilitate workshops, stakeholder meetings, and requirement walkthrough sessions.
Create and maintain key artifacts such as BRDs, FRDs, SRS, User Stories, process flows, wireframes, Use cases, Project Matrices.
Define and track key performance indicators (KPIs) to measure product success and user satisfaction.
Support project delivery by ensuring requirements is clearly understood and implemented correctly.
Lead and mentor Project team. Participate in Agile ceremonies and contribute to continuous improvement processes.
Lead sprint reviews, backlog grooming, and product demos to showcase progress and gather feedback.
Contribute to process improvements, reusable assets, and domain knowledge repositories.
Ensure adherence to delivery timelines, quality standards, and client expectations.

Required Skills and Qualifications
Bachelor’s or master’s degree in computer science, or a related field.
6–8 years of experience as a Product Owner/ Business Analyst in a service-based IT environment.
Strong experience in requirement elicitation, analysis, and documentation.
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to influence stakeholders at all levels.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Proficiency in tools such as JIRA, Confluence, Visio, Balsamiq, or similar.
Ability to translate technical information into business context and vice versa.
Proven experience in pre-sales activities, including solutioning and proposal development.
Experience working with US/onsite clients and handling client communications independently.
Hands-on experience with Agile/Scrum and/or Waterfall methodologies.
Strong understanding of SDLC and software delivery lifecycle.
Ability to lead BA activities across projects.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
Preferred Qualifications
Certifications such as Certified Scrum Product Owner (CSPO), SAFe Product Owner/Manager, or equivalent.
Experience in software or technology-focused companies.
Experience in domains such as HealthCare and Med Tech or Enterprise Applications.
Exposure to digital transformation, cloud-based solutions, or API-driven architectures.
Experience working in multi-geography distributed teams.
Basic understanding of UX/UI principles and wireframing tools.
Soft Skills
Excellent communication and presentation abilities, especially with global clients.
High level of ownership, accountability, and proactive problem-solving.
Ability to work in a fast-paced, dynamic environment with multiple priorities.
Strong analytical thinking and attention to detail.
Team leadership mindset with the ability to mentor and influence without authority.

occupations.MGTWW: Business Management, Administrative & Customer Support Occupations

description.text:
Job Location Bangalore
Work Timings 4:30 PM IST
Qualification MBA,BBA or B.Tech IT
Experience 20+ years
About Brickwork India:

Brickwork India founded in 2005 is a global leader in providing Admin and Business Support assistance. Brickwork has experience in supporting diverse clientele ranging from Fortune 500 companies, Mid-size companies, Start-ups and busy CXOs from across 179 countries. With a strong focus on Business innovation, Customer excellence, and People practices, Brickwork has received numerous awards, including the “Great Place to Work”, “Customer Obsession” award recognised by CII, and has also received extensive media attention through more than 100 publications including the 2 New York Times bestsellers, “The World is Flat” by Thomas Friedman and "The Four-Hour Work Week" by Tim Ferriss. To know more about Brickwork, check the URL: https://www.brickworkindia.com/.

Position Purpose:

We are seeking a seasoned Business Development Head with 20+years of progressive experience in Sales, Marketing, and Business Development across IT and Non-IT Services and Product businesses. The role requires strong exposure to global markets, particularly the USA, with proven expertise in building, leading, and scaling cross-border sales and marketing teams.

The incumbent will be responsible for revenue growth, market expansion, strategic partnerships, and end-to-end business development across multiple geographies

Primary Responsibilities:

Competencies / Skills:

1. Strategic Leadership

· Define and execute global business development strategy aligned with organizational goals.

· Drive market penetration strategy with primary focus on USA market.

· Identify new business verticals, service lines, and product-market fit opportunities.

· Develop annual revenue targets and long-term growth roadmap.

2. Revenue & Sales Management

· Own and deliver P&L responsibility for global sales operations.

· Lead enterprise sales, solution selling, and consultative sales engagements.

· Develop key accounts and build CXO-level relationships across industries.

· Oversee pipeline management, forecasting, and revenue assurance.

3. USA Market Expansion

· Establish and expand footprint in the USA through strategic partnerships and direct sales.

· Build channel networks, alliances, and referral ecosystems.

· Understand regulatory, compliance, and market dynamics specific to USA.

· Drive onsite-offshore business models where applicable.

Marketing & Brand Positioning

· Lead global marketing initiatives including digital, events, content, and demand generation.

· Align marketing strategy with sales objectives to enhance lead conversion ratios.

· Oversee branding, positioning, and go-to-market strategies for services and products.

· Analyze market intelligence and competitor positioning.

5. Team Leadership & Global Coordination

· Manage and mentor sales and marketing teams across multiple countries.

· Set KPIs, performance metrics, and incentive structures.

· Build high-performance culture across geographically distributed teams.

· Drive collaboration between delivery, operations, and sales units.

6. Partnerships & Alliances

· Identify and onboard strategic alliances, channel partners, and technology partners.

· Negotiate and close high-value contracts and strategic deals.

· Represent the organization in global trade forums and industry events.

7. Governance & Reporting

· Implement structured CRM, reporting, and governance mechanisms.

· Present growth strategy and performance metrics to executive leadership.

· Ensure compliance with international trade and contractual norms.

description.text:
Location: Bangalore (requirement to travel to Vellore once a month)

Job Overview:
We are seeking a highly motivated to lead the development and enhancement of our Fixed Income Reference Data (Structured products). The ideal candidate needs to possess deep expertise in fixed income markets and data management, with the ability to translate market needs into scalable product offerings. This role requires a blend of strategic thinking, strong analytical skills, and excellent communication abilities to collaborate effectively with stakeholders and cross-functional teams. We are looking for candidates with prior experience in the financial services industry.

Key Responsibilities:
Product Strategy & Roadmap
Define, and execute the product strategy and roadmap for Fixed Income Structured products offering.
Stay informed about market trends and client needs to identify opportunities for innovation and growth.
Prioritize product features and enhancements based on business value and customer feedback.
Identify gaps in the current product suite and propose solutions to address them.
Product Development & Stakeholder Engagement
Collaborate and engage with the SIX data strategy and stakeholders.
Lead data design, development and delivery of high-quality structured product database and features.
Define clear product requirements, including data attributes, coverage, and delivery formats.
Ensure timely delivery of products that meet performance and quality standards.
Oversee the quality and accuracy of fixed income reference data, ensuring it complies with industry and regulatory standards.
Work with data providers and internal teams to enhance data integrity and coverage.
Engage with clients to gather feedback, validate use cases, and ensure alignment with their needs.
Reporting & Metrics
Define and track key performance indicators (KPIs) to measure product success and user satisfaction.
Provide regular updates to senior management on product performance, market positioning, and revenue growth.
Regulatory Knowledge
Stay updated on global regulations impacting fixed income markets (e.g., MiFID II, Basel III).
Ensure that the product complies with all relevant regulatory requirements.

Key Skills:
Expertise in fixed income markets, securities, and reference data attributes (e.g., identifiers, ratings, maturities) with a focus on Structured products reference data modelling
Strong project management skills
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Ability to define clear product requirements and prioritize features effectively.
Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills
Experience with product lifecycle management tools and data platforms (e.g., Bloomberg, Refinitiv, FactSet).
Key Qualifications:
Education: Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Business Administration,or a related field.
Experience:
12+ years of experience in the financial services industry.
Proven experience in product management, specifically in fixed income markets or financial data products.
Strong understanding of fixed income instruments, pricing, analytics, and reference data.
