description.text:
Job Title
Global Product Manager - Respiratory Therapies

Job Description

Global Product Manager - Respiratory Therapies

Philips is a global health technology leader, focused on improving lives through meaningful innovation. Within our Connected Care business, we deliver solutions that connect patients and providers for better outcomes and experiences.

This role belongs to Sleep & Respiratory Care (SRC) , which provides therapies and technologies for sleep apnea and respiratory conditions. SRC helps patients manage chronic conditions and avoid hospital readmissions, improving quality of life worldwide. The position is based in Pune , a center for innovation and collaboration.

As the Global Product Manager in Business Category, you will support the Category’s product strategy, product requirements for product, solution, and services development, launch, and life-cycle management, or their enhancements, driven by global and local market needs, a deep understanding of customer needs and commercial success criteria. Products are made up of equipment and/or software applications and/or services and/or accessories and disposables.

You are responsible for

Assisting in defining, launching, and building profitable products, solutions, and platforms that improve people’s lives.

Supporting the Life Cycle Management for the existing portfolio from start to end, including identifying opportunities to extend the lifecycle.

Acting as a subject matter expert for the domain, with the ability to clearly convey key messages to cross-functional partners.

Assisting the project team in the execution phase of engineering or design-related changes.

Being the Voice of the Customer and Patient within the development team, defining requirements, and driving alignment of business and feature/function prioritization to maximize the value proposition.

Obtaining global customer requirements and product specifications via market analysis and ongoing communication with key internal and external partners.

Demonstrating a deep understanding of the customers and patients and a thorough understanding of the global marketplace.

Interacting on a regular basis with our marketing teams in key markets to ensure successful management of devices that fit with the local market needs.

Maintaining and evolving key marketing documents and communications to set expectations and enable successful alignment.

To succeed in this role, you should have the following skills and experience

Bachelor’s degree in marketing, management or engineering-related field

8+ Years of over all experience in industry working in similar domain and environment. .

A highly motivated and positive-minded individual with a passion for improving lives.

2-3 years of prior experience in product management, marketing, operations, or sales.

Experience in product development and management.

Knowledge of marketing processes, strategic planning, and budgeting.

Ability to network and influence in a global organization.

Comfortable working across cultures.

Strong decision-making skills based on market and customer feedback.

Excellent communication skills.

Willingness to travel up to 10% domestically and globally.

How we work together
We believe that we are better together than apart. This is an office-based role, which means working in-person at least 3 days per week.

About Philips
We are a health technology company. We built our entire company around the belief that every human matters, and we won't stop until everybody everywhere has access to the quality healthcare that we all deserve. Do the work of your life to help the lives of others.
• Learn more about our business .
• Discover our rich and exciting history .
• Learn more about our purpose .
If you’re interested in this role and have many, but not all, of the experiences needed, we encourage you to apply. You may still be the right candidate for this or other opportunities at Philips. Learn more about our culture of impact with care here .

#LI-EU
#LI-Hybrid
#ConnectedCare

description.text:
Job Title
Project Manager

Job Description

#EOS

#LIPHILIN

Job title:
Project Manager

Your role:

Job Responsibilities:

Lead and execute large‑scale, cross‑functional programs supporting global sourcing strategies, lifecycle initiatives, and long‑term business continuity objectives.
Own end‑to‑end program management, including scope definition, planning, scheduling, governance, dependency management, risk & issue management, and successful delivery.
Drive lifecycle sustainability and risk‑mitigation initiatives to ensure continuity of supply, product availability, and compliance across the portfolio.
Support and manage business case development, ensuring alignment with financial targets, value realization, and strategic priorities.
Develop and maintain program structures, including WBS, RACI, milestones, effort estimation, and resource planning.
Ensure delivery against critical KPIs related to quality, cost, productivity, delivery, and long‑term assurance of supply across the product lifecycle, aligned with annual business objectives.
Coordinate closely with engineering and technical teams to establish technical requirements, feasibility assessments, and risk evaluations for programs and initiatives.
Lead cross‑functional reviews with internal and external stakeholders to track progress, review metrics, address deviations, and drive corrective actions.
Manage scope changes, priorities, and trade‑offs, ensuring alignment with agreed business objectives and stakeholder expectations.
Ensure adherence to corporate, quality, and regulatory requirements, maintaining strong governance and audit‑ready documentation.
Promote continuous improvement by leveraging best practices, lessons learned, and advancements in project and program management tools and methodologies.

You're the right fit if:

Minimum required Education:
Bachelor’s or master’s degree in electrical, Electronics Engineering, or equivalent technical discipline / master's degree in project management.

Minimum required Experience:

12+ years of experience in a high‑tech or regulated industry, with strong exposure to program or senior project management.

Minimum required Certification:

PMP (Project Management Professional) certification

OR

Philips Project Management Expert / Practitioner certification

Preferred Experience:

Demonstrated experience leading complex, multi‑disciplinary programs across engineering, supply chain, quality, procurement, and manufacturing functions.
Proven ability to manage lifecycle‑driven and sustaining initiatives, balancing innovation with long‑term portfolio continuity.
Strong track record of influencing senior stakeholders and driving execution in matrixed, global environments.
Familiarity with project management and reporting tools such as MS Project, Smartsheets, Power BI, or equivalent.
Hands‑on experience with engineering change management, design transfer, or industrialization processes preferred.
Experience in design/development of electrical/electronic modules like battery. power supplies, displays/PCBAs would be an added advantag
Exposure to regulated environments (e.g., medical devices, healthcare, or equivalent) is a strong advantage

Preferred Skills:

Program & Senior‑level Project Management
Stakeholder Management and Influence without Authority
Business Acumen and Value‑based Decision Making
Risk, Issue, and Dependency Management
Governance, Documentation, and Executive Reporting
Cross‑functional and Global Team Leadership
Quality, Compliance, and Lifecycle Mindset
Ownership, Accountability, and Delivery Focus

How we work together
We believe that we are better together than apart. For our office-based teams, this means working in-person at least 3 days per week.
Onsite roles require full-time presence in the company’s facilities.
Field roles are most effectively done outside of the company’s main facilities, generally at the customers’ or suppliers’ locations.
Indicate if this role is an office/field/onsite role.
About Philips
We are a health technology company. We built our entire company around the belief that every human matters, and we won't stop until everybody everywhere has access to the quality healthcare that we all deserve. Do the work of your life to help the lives of others.
• Learn more about our business .
• Discover our rich and exciting history .
• Learn more about our purpose .
If you’re interested in this role and have many, but not all, of the experiences needed, we encourage you to apply. You may still be the right candidate for this or other opportunities at Philips. Learn more about our culture of impact with care here .

description.text:
Job Title
Senior Specialist I - DevOps Security

Job Description

Job Responsibilities:

Engages in solution-level DevOps and cloud security processes, collaborating closely with peers and engineering teams to ensure effective, secure implementation while fostering knowledge sharing and cross-functional collaboration.

Streamlines software development cycles and feedback loops through automation and orchestration, enabling rapid, secure release of high-quality software products.

Provides and supports essential DevOps tooling and infrastructure, including source control, CI/CD pipelines, and quality and security analysis tools, ensuring development processes are efficient, scalable, and aligned with security best practices.

Executes, evaluates, and interprets security scans and findings using AWS-native services such as AWS GuardDuty, AWS Inspector, AWS Security Hub, and similar, and performs downstream analysis of SIEM-generated findings in partnership with centralized security teams.

Assesses vulnerabilities and CVEs affecting cloud infrastructure, pipelines, and applications, guiding prioritization and remediation while promoting shared ownership of security tasks across teams.

Develops, maintains, and improves automated build and deployment workflows using infrastructure-as-code and scripting, reducing manual effort while strengthening security controls, auditability, and reliability.

Demonstrated ability to translate security recommendations into real‑world DevOps implementations, including designing, automating, and operating secure cloud infrastructure, CI/CD pipelines, and platform services using hands‑on engineering expertise.

Collaborates with engineering teams on code releases and production support, providing guidance during issue resolution and helping balance delivery velocity with security and stability considerations.

Advocates for DevOps and cloud security best practices across the software development lifecycle, driving security awareness, continuous improvement, and consistent adoption of approved patterns.

Implements and leverages telemetry and monitoring solutions to gain operational and security insights, proactively identifying risks and opportunities to improve system performance, reliability, and security posture.

Partner closely with other security teams across the organization to align on cloud security initiatives, control requirements, and risk remediation strategies; translate security expectations into actionable DevOps implementations.

Minimum required Education:
Bachelor's / Master's Degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, Information Technology or equivalent.

Minimum required Experience:
Minimum 4-5 years of hands‑on experience in DevOps, SRE, or platform engineering roles, with at least 3 years focused on security practices in cloud-native or DevSecOps environments.

Preferred Education:
Master’s degree in a relevant technical discipline and/or industry certifications in cloud platforms, security, or DevSecOps practices.

Preferred Skills:

DevOps / DevSecOps

CI/CD

Infrastructure as Code

Cloud Security

IAM & Secrets

Logging & SIEM

Vulnerability Management

Automation & Scripting

AI‑Assisted Engineering

SDLC & Agile

Troubleshooting

Business Acumen

How we work together
We believe that we are better together than apart. For our office-based teams, this means working in-person at least 3 days per week.
Onsite roles require full-time presence in the company’s facilities.
Field roles are most effectively done outside of the company’s main facilities, generally at the customers’ or suppliers’ locations.
Indicate if this role is an office/field/onsite role.

description.text:
Job Title
Senior Engineering Manager – MR R&D

Job Description

Senior Engineering Manager – MR R&D HW Components, Actuation

The Senior Engineering Manager is part of the Global MR R&D organization within the Hardware / firmware and Embedded programming Components Actuation & Controls team. This role leads a high-performing, multidisciplinary group of embedded control, electrical, and Electronics engineers (including designers and architects) and delivers complex programs that strengthen MR platform capabilities, improve customer outcomes, and support profitability. A key focus is to build and evolve the Actuation & Controls organization and technology portfolio to meet future customer and product-platform needs.

Your Role:

Own end-to-end development of Actuation & Controls hardware / firmware and Embedded programming and control solutions—from concept and design through verification/validation, manufacturing transfer, and lifecycle support.

Lead and develop a multidisciplinary engineering team across control, electrical/electronics, Electronics, and mechatronics domains.

Define the technical roadmap and innovation pipeline for Actuation & Controls, aligned to system requirements and future MR platforms.

Provide technical leadership in control architecture, system integration, and supplier transitions to ensure performance, safety, and compliance.

Build and scale Excellence for control, electrical/electronics, and mechatronics engineering (architects, leads, engineers, and technicians).

Partner cross-functionally with Systems, Verification, Quality, Regulatory, Supply Chain, Service, Clinical, and Marketing teams.

Lead supplier transfer and risk-mitigation programs to ensure supply continuity and develop alternate sources for high-risk components.

Ensure adherence to regulatory standards (e.g., IEC, FDA, UL, CE), internal QMS processes, and country-specific compliance requirements.

Drive BOM cost and productivity improvements through design optimization and alternate sourcing strategies.

Inculcate Digital mindset for AI adoption & analysis first approach within team.

Define subsystem architecture within platform and legacy constraints; advise system architects on HW/SW partitioning and make-versus-buy decisions.

Own project planning and execution, including effort estimation, WBS/RACI definition, resourcing, and schedule tracking.

Drive technical reviews and quality practices (DFMEA, DFM/DFA, RMM) to ensure robust designs and effective risk controls.

Support customer escalations and field/service issues through structured root-cause analysis and timely corrective actions.

Maintain reliability, safety, and serviceability standards across the full product lifecycle.

Ensure consistent application of engineering processes, regulatory policies, and Philips governance.

Own functional management responsibilities include hiring, coaching, performance management, and capability/competency development.

Foster a culture of innovation, ownership, and strong cross-functional collaboration.

Drive continuous improvement in engineering practices, product quality, cost, and delivery performance.

Proactively identify and manage technical and program risks, define mitigations, and drive issues to closure.

Stay current with relevant industry standards and contribute to their adoption and continuous improvement.

You are fit if:

Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Electrical, Electronics, mechatronics, Electronics Engineering, or a related discipline.

15+ years of experience in end-to-end product development and lifecycle management, including sustaining engineering.

Demonstrated people leadership with a track record of building and sustaining high performance.

Strong experience in actuation and control hardware / firmware and Embedded programming development; medical device or imaging experience is a strong advantage.

Ability to lead multidisciplinary teams and manage complex development programs across the full product lifecycle.

Strong knowledge of control principles, system-level integration, and product safety/compliance requirements.

Proficiency with simulation tools (e.g. ECAD tools (e.g., Altium, Mentor Graphics etc), and lab/test equipment (e.g., VNA, spectrum analyzer).

Proficiency with Embedded Programming ( C, C++) & Communication Protocols like Ethernet, CAN, I2C etc.

Working knowledge of relevant standards and regulations, including IEC 60601, IEC 61010, FDA, CE, and QMS frameworks.

Track record in supplier transitions, BOM optimization, and design-for-manufacturing practices.

Hands-on experience with engineering change management (ECM) and design transfer processes.

Experience with system testing, test automation, and test fixture design.

Experience with industrial/supply-base transfer projects and supplier relationship management.

Experience working in a matrixed, global environment.

Strong stakeholder management, communication, and leadership skills.

Strong customer focus and passion for quality, reliability, and continuous improvement.

Strong problem-solving skills, including structured root-cause analysis methodologies.

Collaborative, inclusive leadership style with strong interpersonal skills and a Lean/efficiency mindset.

How we work together:
We believe that we are better together than apart. For our office-based teams, this means working in-person at least 3 days per week.

Onsite roles require full-time presence in the company’s facilities.

Field roles are most effectively done outside of the company’s main facilities, generally at the customers’ or suppliers’ locations.

This role is an office-based role

About Philips:
We are a health technology company. We built our entire company around the belief that every human matters, and we won't stop until everybody everywhere has access to the quality healthcare that we all deserve. Do the work of your life to help the lives of others.

  • Build and scale Excellence for control, electrical/electronics, and mechatronics engineering (architects, leads, engineers, and technicians).

  • Partner cross-functionally with Systems, Verification, Quality, Regulatory, Supply Chain, Service, Clinical, and Marketing teams.

  • Lead supplier transfer and risk-mitigation programs to ensure supply continuity and develop alternate sources for high-risk components.

  • Ensure adherence to regulatory standards (e.g., IEC, FDA, UL, CE), internal QMS processes, and country-specific compliance requirements.

  • Drive BOM cost and productivity improvements through design optimization and alternate sourcing strategies.

  • Inculcate Digital mindset for AI adoption & analysis first approach within team.

  • Define subsystem architecture within platform and legacy constraints; advise system architects on HW/SW partitioning and make-versus-buy decisions.

  • Own project planning and execution, including effort estimation, WBS/RACI definition, resourcing, and schedule tracking.

  • Drive technical reviews and quality practices (DFMEA, DFM/DFA, RMM) to ensure robust designs and effective risk controls.

  • Support customer escalations and field/service issues through structured root-cause analysis and timely corrective actions.

  • Maintain reliability, safety, and serviceability standards across the full product lifecycle.

  • Ensure consistent application of engineering processes, regulatory policies, and Philips governance.

  • Own functional management responsibilities include hiring, coaching, performance management, and capability/competency development.

  • Foster a culture of innovation, ownership, and strong cross-functional collaboration.

  • Drive continuous improvement in engineering practices, product quality, cost, and delivery performance.

  • Proactively identify and manage technical and program risks, define mitigations, and drive issues to closure.

  • Stay current with relevant industry standards and contribute to their adoption and continuous improvement.

You are fit if:

  • Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Electrical, Electronics, mechatronics, Electronics Engineering, or a related discipline.

  • 15+ years of experience in end-to-end product development and lifecycle management, including sustaining engineering.

  • Demonstrated people leadership with a track record of building and sustaining high performance.

  • Strong experience in actuation and control hardware / firmware and Embedded programming development; medical device or imaging experience is a strong advantage.

  • Ability to lead multidisciplinary teams and manage complex development programs across the full product lifecycle.

  • Strong knowledge of control principles, system-level integration, and product safety/compliance requirements.

  • Proficiency with simulation tools (e.g. ECAD tools (e.g., Altium, Mentor Graphics etc), and lab/test equipment (e.g., VNA, spectrum analyzer).

  • Proficiency with Embedded Programming ( C, C++) & Communication Protocols like Ethernet, CAN, I2C etc.

  • Working knowledge of relevant standards and regulations, including IEC 60601, IEC 61010, FDA, CE, and QMS frameworks.

  • Track record in supplier transitions, BOM optimization, and design-for-manufacturing practices.

  • Hands-on experience with engineering change management (ECM) and design transfer processes.

  • Experience with system testing, test automation, and test fixture design.

  • Experience with industrial/supply-base transfer projects and supplier relationship management.

  • Experience working in a matrixed, global environment.

  • Strong stakeholder management, communication, and leadership skills.

  • Strong customer focus and passion for quality, reliability, and continuous improvement.

  • Strong problem-solving skills, including structured root-cause analysis methodologies.

  • Collaborative, inclusive leadership style with strong interpersonal skills and a Lean/efficiency mindset.

How we work together:
We believe that we are better together than apart. For our office-based teams, this means working in-person at least 3 days per week.

Onsite roles require full-time presence in the company’s facilities.

Field roles are most effectively done outside of the company’s main facilities, generally at the customers’ or suppliers’ locations.

This role is an office-based role

About Philips:
We are a health technology company. We built our entire company around the belief that every human matters, and we won't stop until everybody everywhere has access to the quality healthcare that we all deserve. Do the work of your life to help the lives of others.

description.text:
Job Title
Technical Project Manager (STET)

Job Description

#EOS

#LIPHILIN

Job title:
Technical Project Manager (STET)
Your role:

Job Responsibilities:

Leads medium to large, cross-functional procurement /productivity programs, including supplier transfers, lifecycle management initiatives, regulatory compliance / quality related projects and/or cost optimization programs across global sites.
Manage lifecycle changes and transition activities to ensure continuity of supply, avoiding line stoppages, and minimizing business disruption during change execution.
Owns end-to-end savings funnel governance, including opportunity identification, validation, SMRS tracking, EBITA impact assessment, and risk-adjusted savings realization, in collaboration with procurement and finance stakeholders.
Ensures project execution aligns with quality, regulatory, and compliance requirements (ISO 13485, FDA QMSR, EU MDR where applicable), maintaining audit-ready documentation and governance.
Participates in phase-in and phase-out (PIPO) planning related to new product introduction and lifecycle management (LCM) activities, collaborating with cross-functional teams to ensure seamless transitions and alignment with procurement goals.
Executes comprehensive project management plans, outlining project scope, objectives, deliverables, schedules, and resource allocations to guide project execution and control.
Implements project activities, overseeing project progress, resolving issues, and ensuring adherence to project plans and procurement standards.
Identifies and resolves project issues and risks, escalating critical issues to project stakeholders and management as necessary to minimize disruptions and mitigate project impacts.
Provides regular project updates and reports to project stakeholders, communicating project status, milestones, risks, and issues to ensure transparency and alignment with project objectives.
Drafts project documentation and records, including project plans, meeting minutes, and status reports, ensuring accuracy, completeness, and accessibility for project team members and stakeholders.
Ensures compliance with procurement policies, procedures, and regulations, adhering to ethical standards and promoting a culture of integrity and transparency within the procurement function.
Understands and applies best practices in project management and procurement, continuously learning and adapting to evolving industry trends, technologies, and methodologies to drive procurement excellence.

.
You're the right fit if:

Minimum required Education:
Bachelor’s degree in mechanical or Equivalent Engineering

Minimum required Experience:
7–12 years of experience in project/program management within procurement, supply chain, NPI/LCM, or productivity programs, preferably in a regulated manufacturing or medical devices environment.

Minimum required Certification:
PMP Certification/ Philips Project Management practitioner

Preferred Experience:
Proven experience in leading lifecycle management initiatives (NPI, PIPO, LCM) and end-to-end supplier collaboration for part development, qualification, cost optimization, and localization/transfer projects within a regulated manufacturing environment.

Preferred Skills:

• Stakeholder Partnership
• Emotional Intelligence
• Ownership and Commitment
• Customer Centricity
• Judgement
• Learning Agility
• Business Acumen
• Servant Leadership
• End‑to‑end Project Management execution across cross‑functional teams
• Documentation & Reporting
• Procurement Policies and Processes
• Cost Optimization and Savings Tracking

How we work together
We believe that we are better together than apart. For our office-based teams, this means working in-person at least 3 days per week.
Onsite roles require full-time presence in the company’s facilities.
Field roles are most effectively done outside of the company’s main facilities, generally at the customers’ or suppliers’ locations.
Indicate if this role is an office/field/onsite role.
About Philips
We are a health technology company. We built our entire company around the belief that every human matters, and we won't stop until everybody everywhere has access to the quality healthcare that we all deserve. Do the work of your life to help the lives of others.
• Learn more about our business .
• Discover our rich and exciting history .
• Learn more about our purpose .
If you’re interested in this role and have many, but not all, of the experiences needed, we encourage you to apply. You may still be the right candidate for this or other opportunities at Philips. Learn more about our culture of impact with care here .

description.text:
Role : Senior Software Quality Assurance Analyst
Location : Bengaluru

Overview :

We are seeking a Senior Software Quality Assurance Analyst to lead QA strategy and execution for complex, enterprise applications. You will define testing approaches, manage end-to-end and regression testing, drive quality improvements across the SDLC in an Agile environment, and mentor junior QAs. Strong experience with test management tools, defect tracking, and enterprise integrations (Pega experience preferred) is required.

As a Senior Software Quality Assurance Analyst, you will be a quality leader and hands-on tester responsible for ensuring that complex features and system integrations meet business and technical requirements. You will define and implement testing strategies and standards, lead end-to-end and regression testing for major releases, and embed shift-left practices to catch defects early. Working closely with product owners, developers, and business analysts, you will translate user stories into thorough test coverage, run manual and exploratory tests, and ensure traceability between requirements, test cases, and defects.

Key responsibilities:

Lead QA initiatives and continuous process improvements; establish and maintain testing standards and quality metrics
Define and implement testing strategies for complex features, integrations, and end-to-end business processes
Analyze user stories and requirements; design, write, and execute comprehensive sprint and regression test cases
Convert sprint test cases to regression suite artifacts and maintain organized test case management in ALM Octane (or similar)
Perform static testing during estimations and grooming; estimate testing effort and complexity for stories
Lead release-level end-to-end testing and coordinate cross-system integration testing
Log, track, and prioritize defects in VersionOne / Digital.AI (or similar); participate in defect triage and drive resolution
Participate actively in Agile/Scrum ceremonies (planning, daily standups, retrospectives, demos) and collaborate on backlog refinement
Mentor and train junior QA analysts on testing techniques, tools, and best practices; promote a culture of quality and continuous learning
Communicate testing status, risks, and issues to stakeholders; facilitate knowledge-sharing and lessons-learned sessions

Required Qualification:

5+ years of progressive experience in software quality assurance, including leading testing efforts for enterprise applications
Proven experience working in Agile/Scrum environments and influencing quality across teams
Hands-on experience with test case management tools (ALM Octane or similar) and defect tracking/project management tools (VersionOne / Digital.AI or similar)
Strong knowledge of testing methodologies, test design techniques, requirements analysis, and static testing
Experience testing complex integrations, end-to-end processes, and domain knowledge with Order Management or similar business applications
Excellent analytical, problem-solving, communication, and collaboration skills; ability to manage multiple priorities and drive results
Bachelor’s degree in computer science, IT, or related field, or equivalent experience

Preferred qualifications:

UI automation experience with Playwright (JavaScript) or similar frameworks (Selenium, Cypress)
API automation experience with Playwright, Postman, or equivalent tools
Programming/scripting knowledge in JavaScript and experience integrating automated tests into CI/CD pipelines
Pega platform experience highly preferred
If you are a proactive, quality-focused leader who enjoys mentoring others and improving testing practices across teams, this role offers the opportunity to shape QA strategy for complex enterprise systems and ensure reliable delivery of business-critical functionality

description.text:
Role : Software Quality Assurance Analyst
Location : Bengaluru

Overview

We are seeking a detail-oriented and analytical Senior Quality Assurance (QA) Analyst to join our Business Process Workflow Services team. The Senior QA Analyst will play a critical role in ensuring the accuracy, efficiency, and reliability of business process workflows across various platforms and systems. This role involves close collaboration with business analysts, developers, and stakeholders to validate process designs, identify defects, and ensure high-quality deliverables that meet business requirements.

Job Responsibilities

Test Planning & Execution
Develop comprehensive test plans, test cases, and test scripts based on business process requirements and workflow designs
Execute manual and automated testing across workflow applications and platforms

Validate end-to-end business processes for accuracy, completeness, and performance

Defect Management
Identify, document, and track defects using standard QA tools
Collaborate with development teams to troubleshoot and resolve issues

Participate in root cause analysis and recommend process improvements

QA Team Oversight
Lead junior team members

Work with QA Leads to standards and process methods are improved and maintained

Process Validation
Ensure workflows align with business rules, compliance standards, and operational goals
Perform regression testing to validate changes and enhancements

Support user acceptance testing (UAT) and coordinate with stakeholders to sign-off

Documentation & Reporting
Maintain detailed QA documentation including test results, defect logs, and quality metrics
Provide regular status updates and quality reports to project managers and leadership
Contribute to QA best practices and continuous improvement initiatives

Job Requirements

Education & Experience
Bachelor’s degree in computer science, Information Systems, Business Administration, or related field
5+ years of experience in quality assurance or testing, preferably in business process or workflow environments

Technical Skills
Proficiency in QA tools such as Jira, Octane, or other similar
Experience with workflow platforms (e.g., Appian, Pega, ServiceNow, or similar)
Familiarity with automation tools and scripting languages

Analytical & Communication Skills
Strong analytical skills with attention to detail
Excellent written and verbal communication skills
Ability to work collaboratively in cross-functional teams

Other Qualifications
QA Team leader responsibilities
Knowledge of business process modeling and documentation standards
Understanding of Agile and Waterfall methodologies
Certification in QA or testing (e.g., ISTQB) is desirable

employer.briefDescription: At Aptiv we’re working to make the world a safer, greener and more connected place.That’s how together, we’re going to change what tomorrow means.

description.text:
SDET Engineer – Python, Linux, Kubernetes Testing

ABOUT WIND RIVER

Wind River is a global leader in delivering software for mission-critical intelligent systems. For more than four decades, the company has been an innovator and pioneer, powering billions of systems that require the highest levels of security, safety, and reliability. Wind River helps customers across automotive, aerospace, defense, industrial, medical, and telecommunications industries solve complex technology challenges on their journey toward the new intelligent machine economy.

The company’s software powers generation after generation of the safest, most secure systems in the world. Examples include playing a key role in NASA space missions — such as Artemis I, the James Webb Space Telescope, and multiple Mars rovers — and in recent 5G milestones — including the world’s first successful 5G data session with Verizon and in building one of the largest Open RAN networks in the world with Vodafone.

The company has received industry recognition for its technology innovation and leadership, and for its workplace culture, including global Great Place to Work certification and being named a “Top Workplace” for ten consecutive years. If you want to be part of a unique culture where the lived experience is based on our cultural attributes of growth mindset, customer focus, and diversity, equity, inclusion & belonging, come join us and help advance the future software-defined world.

Wind River Systems is seeking a Engineer– Certification Center of Excellence our Professional Services Safety Certification team in Bangalore, India. This high-performing engineer will be a critical member of a co-located team generating software and evidence for safety critical systems. You will interact with our highly skilled global teams in North America or Europe on a regular basis:

Your Role

Develop system tests and execution of system test cases for a highly available, carrier grade platform which will be the platform of choice for 5G vendors
Work with the most dynamic and exciting open source software projects today – Kubernetes (K8s), Docker, Helm, Openstack, Ansible, Ceph, etc
Work as part of a team focused on containerization, virtualization, VM management and fault tolerant/carrier grade testing.
Investigate and report issues during product verification cycles
Provide support to documentation and customer-facing teams
Participate in all aspects of the software development process using agile development

Your Background

Key ingredients for succeeding in this role are your:

BSc / MSc degree (Computer Science, Computer/Electrical Engineering, or equivalent technical degree) More than 1-3 years of relevant technical experience in software development and testing automation
#EXP[1-3]
Experience in software test automation and developing test automation framework from scratch
Experience with Linux environment
Strong programming skills using Python with different frameworks (Robot, Pytest etc.) and Plugins
Strong analytical and debugging skills
Hands on experience with Elastic Stack verification
Exposure to back-end and UI automation
Excellent written and oral communication skills
Exceptional team player who works well in collaborative environments
Exposure to other languages (Shell Scripting) would be an asset
Understanding of other domains such as storage/security/DC would be a plus
Self-managed, fast learner with the desire and ability to master new technologies
Experience with containers and/or cloud-based technologies including Kubernetes, OpenStack would be an asset
Experience with industry standard networking test tools would be an asset
A good level of understanding / contribution to the open source community would be an asset

#LI-NC1

Privacy Notice - Active Candidates: https://www.aptiv.com/privacy-notice-active-candidates

Aptiv is an equal employment opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, disability status, protected veteran status or any other characteristic protected by law.

description.text:
Company Description

About us:

Passion for food. Hunger for tech. We make METRO digital.

Today technology is driving the world. And at METRO.digital we are driving the technology for one of the leading international wholesalers specializing in food - METRO. From e-commerce to checkout, to delivery software, we work on a wide range of products to make each day a success for our customers and colleagues. With passion and ownership, we build the future of wholesale.

You are driving to create smart solutions for customers around the globe? You want to grow in a flexible environment? Let the right career opportunity find you and join us!

Job Description

Your Responsibilities:

Collaborate within a cross-functional product team and with other teams to solve customer problems by delivering high-quality product functionality and operational support.
Participate in all phases of the software engineering lifecycle, including ideation, design, implementation, testing, deployment, and ongoing operation.
Develop high-quality, maintainable code in accordance with established best practices and clean code principles.
Proactively advocate for and contribute to improvements in product quality, security, reliability, and performance.
Collaborate with engineers and other team members across multiple layers of the infrastructure to design, implement, and launch new features.
Engage with stakeholders to understand, clarify, and help shape their requirements, providing technical guidance and recommendations where appropriate.

Required Key Competencies and Qualifications

Profound expertise in GitHub Actions and GitHub Workflows, as well as strong programming skills in TypeScript and Bash.
Demonstrated passion for automating, scaling, and optimizing test and deployment processes, including hands-on experience with Docker and Kubernetes.
Strong commitment to implementing state-of-the-art DevOps practices within CI/CD and deployment pipelines.
Ability and willingness to consult and collaborate with cross-functional teams on software delivery pipelines, and to share best practices in modern software delivery.
Enjoyment of solving complex technical and business challenges in a collaborative, high-performing team environment.
Strong motivation for continuous learning and professional development, with a desire to contribute to meaningful improvements in digital products and platforms.
Interest in data processing, analysis, and the calculation and interpretation of relevant metrics.
Curiosity and enthusiasm for exploring, adopting, and applying new technologies and industry trends.
Excellent communication skills in English, both written and verbal.

Qualifications

Strongly Preferred

Hands-on experience with GitHub, including GitHub Workflows, Actions, Apps, and self-hosted runners.
Solid practical knowledge of containerization and orchestration technologies, specifically Docker and Kubernetes.
Experience working with Google Cloud Platform (GCP), with a focus on GKE, Cloud Run Functions, and BigQuery.
Familiarity with GitHub Enterprise Cloud administration and management.
Proficiency in Bash scripting and TypeScript.
Experience creating, maintaining, and deploying Helm charts.

Nice to Have

Experience designing and maintaining dashboards in Datadog.
Knowledge of maintaining Jenkins shared libraries, including Groovy scripting.

title: QA Engineer – NICE Contact Center & CRM Integrations

employer.name: EVERSANA
employer.briefDescription: Together with more than 5,000 team members worldwide, we’ve built an integrated commercial services platform to deliver on our mission to create a healthier world. Join us at eversana.com/careers.

description.text:
Company Description

Company Description

At EVERSANA, we are proud to be certified as a Great Place to Work across the globe. We’re fueled by our vision to create a healthier world. How? Our global team of more than 7,000 employees is committed to creating and delivering next-generation commercialization services to the life sciences industry. We are grounded in our cultural beliefs and serve more than 650 clients ranging from innovative biotech start-ups to established pharmaceutical companies. Our products, services and solutions help bring innovative therapies to market and support the patients who depend on them. Our jobs, skills and talents are unique, but together we make an impact every day. Join us!

Across our growing organization, we embrace diversity in backgrounds and experiences. Improving patient lives around the world is a priority, and we need people from all backgrounds and swaths of life to help build the future of the healthcare and the life sciences industry. We believe our people make all the difference in cultivating an inclusive culture that embraces our cultural beliefs. We are deliberate and self-reflective about the kind of team and culture we are building. We look for team members that are not only strong in their own aptitudes but also who care deeply about EVERSANA, our people, clients and most importantly, the patients we serve. We are EVERSANA.

Job Description

THE POSITION:

The QA Engineer is responsible for validating the quality, stability, and readiness of NICE contact center solutions across voice, digital, omnichannel, and integrated CRM experiences. This role ensures that platform configurations, conversational workflows, and system integrations meet defined requirements, function as designed, and perform reliably in production. By partnering closely with engineers, Business Analysts, and business stakeholders, the QA Engineer helps protect customer experience, agent productivity, and platform integrity across all NICE‑enabled interactions.

ESSENTIAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES:
Our employees are tasked with delivering excellent business results through the efforts of their teams. These results are achieved by:

Develop and execute test strategies, test plans, and test cases for NICE contact center solutions, including voice, digital chat, messaging, omnichannel routing, and agent‑assist capabilities.
Validate end‑to‑end customer and agent journeys across NICE and integrated CRM systems, ensuring correct data flow, context preservation, and workflow behavior.
Perform functional, integration, regression, and release testing for NICE configurations, enhancements, and defect fixes.
Test and validate CRM integrations with NICE, including screen pops, case creation, activity logging, data synchronization, and error handling.
Verify handoffs between automation (Autopilot/Copilot), live agents, and CRM workflows to ensure seamless transitions and accurate context delivery.
Identify, document, and track defects clearly and effectively, working with engineers to support resolution and retesting.
Ensure that test results align with approved requirements, acceptance criteria, and business use cases.
Support user acceptance testing (UAT) activities by assisting with test execution, issue validation, and release sign‑off readiness.
Validate that all solutions meet quality, security, compliance, and performance expectations prior to production release.
Maintain accurate QA documentation, including test artifacts and release validation evidence.All other duties as assigned

It is the policy of EVERSANA to provide reasonable accommodation when requested by an employee with a disability, unless such accommodation would cause an undue hardship for EVERSANA. If reasonable accommodation is needed to perform the essential functions of your job position, please contact Human Resources.

EXPECTATIONS OF THE JOB:

Partner with Business Analysts, engineers, CRM teams, and business stakeholders throughout the delivery lifecycle.
Participate in requirements reviews, design walkthroughs, and release planning sessions to ensure test coverage and clarity.
Communicate testing status, risks, and defects clearly to technical and non‑technical audiences.
Follow established QA, change management, and documentation standards for all NICE‑related initiatives.
Manage multiple testing efforts simultaneously while meeting release timelines.
Support after‑hours or off‑cycle testing as needed for major releases or urgent fixes.
Continuously improve QA practices by identifying testing gaps, tool enhancements, and process improvements.
Maintain a strong production‑quality mindset, prioritizing customer experience and platform reliability.

Qualifications

Experience and/or Training

3–5 years of QA or testing experience supporting enterprise technology platforms.
Experience testing contact center solutions (cloud or on‑prem), including voice or digital channels.
Hands‑on experience testing system integrations, preferably between contact center platforms and CRM systems.
Experience creating and executing test cases based on business and technical requirements.
Familiarity with SDLC and QA practices in Agile or iterative delivery environments.

MINIMUM KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS AND ABILITIES:

The requirements below are representative of the experience, education, knowledge, skills, and abilities required to perform this job effectively.

Education

Bachelor’s degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field, or equivalent practical experience.

Additional Information

All your information will be kept confidential according to EEO guidelines.

Our team is aware of recent fraudulent job offers in the market, misrepresenting EVERSANA. Recruitment fraud is a sophisticated scam commonly perpetrated through online services using fake websites, unsolicited e-mails, or even text messages claiming to be a legitimate company. Some of these scams request personal information and even payment for training or job application fees. Please know EVERSANA would never require personal information nor payment of any kind during the employment process. We respect the personal rights of all candidates looking to explore careers at EVERSANA.

From EVERSANA’s inception, Diversity, Equity & Inclusion have always been key to our success. We are an Equal Opportunity Employer, and our employees are people with different strengths, experiences, and backgrounds who share a passion for improving the lives of patients and leading innovation within the healthcare industry. Diversity not only includes race and gender identity, but also age, disability status, veteran status, sexual orientation, religion, and many other parts of one’s identity. All of our employees’ points of view are key to our success, and inclusion is everyone's responsibility.

Follow us on LinkedIn | Twitter

description.text:
Practice Head-DPO
Job Summary
To provide leadership and guidance in their area of expertise, ensuring compliance with applicable norms and standards. Additionally, this role focuses on training team members, developing business plans, and fostering team talent in alignment with organizational goals.
Key Responsibilities
1. Provide Leadership, Guidance, And Advice In The Area Of Expertise.
2. Ensure Adherence To Applicable Norms And Standards.
3. Conduct Training Sessions For Team Members In Areas Of Expertise.
4. Develop And Interpret Business Plans And Policies For The Team.
5. Communicate Team Viewpoints To Management.
6. Write A Minimum Of One White Paper Per Year On Practice.
7. Nurture And Develop Team Talent Within Organizational Objectives.
Skill Requirements
Other Requirements

Information at a Glance
Why HCLTech?

At HCLTech, you'll supercharge your potential. You'll find your career. And you'll find your spark. All at a place that knows that helping its customers stay on top starts by putting its people first.

HCLTech is a global technology company, home to more than 226,300 people across 60 countries, delivering industry-leading capabilities centered around digital, engineering, cloud and AI, powered by a broad portfolio of technology services and products. We work with clients across all major verticals, providing industry solutions for Financial Services, Manufacturing, Life Sciences and Healthcare, Technology and Services, Telecom and Media, Retail and CPG, and Public Services. Consolidated revenues as of 12 months ending December 2025 totaled $14.5 billion.

Benefits

At HCLTech, we believe in empowering our employees with comprehensive benefits that support their professional growth and enhance their well-being. When you sign up for a career with us, you gain access to:

Industry-benchmarked compensation

Best-in-class healthcare benefits

Personal time off

Maternity and paternity benefits

Access to skills / higher education programs/resources

Discounts on products and services via Benefit Box

Participate in CSR programs and live life with a purpose

Opportunities to grow and advance your career

Note: The benefits listed above vary depending on the nature of your employment and the country where you work. Some benefits may be available in some countries but not in all.

description.text:
Tableau Senior Technical Lead
Job Summary
The Senior Technical Lead in this role is responsible for overseeing the technical aspects related to Tableau and SQL. They will lead a team of technical professionals, ensuring the successful implementation and maintenance of Tableau solutions. The primary objectives include optimizing Tableau performance, designing effective data visualization solutions, and managing SQL databases efficiently.
Key Responsibilities
1. Lead a team of technical professionals to develop, test, and implement tableau dashboards and reports.
2. Ensure the tableau solutions meet the business requirements and adhere to best practices in data visualization.
3. Optimize tableau performance by identifying areas for improvement and implementing necessary changes.
4. Design and develop sql queries, stored procedures, and functions to support data extraction and manipulation.
5. Manage sql databases, ensuring data integrity, security, and performance.
6. Collaborate with stakeholders to understand data requirements and translate them into actionable insights through tableau visualizations.
7. Stay updated on the latest trends and technologies in tableau and sql to recommend enhancements and improvements.
Skill Requirements
1. Proficiency in tableau desktop and server for data visualization and dashboard development.
2. Strong sql skills with the ability to write complex queries, stored procedures, and functions.
3. Experience in optimizing tableau performance and troubleshooting technical issues.
4. Knowledge of database management principles and experience in sql database administration.
5. Strong analytical and problem-solving skills to interpret data and provide valuable insights.
6. Excellent communication skills to collaborate with cross functional teams and stakeholders.
7. Ability to lead a team effectively, prioritize tasks, and meet project deadlines.
Other Requirements
1.Tableau Desktop Certified Professional is preferred.

Information at a Glance
Why HCLTech?

At HCLTech, you'll supercharge your potential. You'll find your career. And you'll find your spark. All at a place that knows that helping its customers stay on top starts by putting its people first.

HCLTech is a global technology company, home to more than 226,300 people across 60 countries, delivering industry-leading capabilities centered around digital, engineering, cloud and AI, powered by a broad portfolio of technology services and products. We work with clients across all major verticals, providing industry solutions for Financial Services, Manufacturing, Life Sciences and Healthcare, Technology and Services, Telecom and Media, Retail and CPG, and Public Services. Consolidated revenues as of 12 months ending December 2025 totaled $14.5 billion.

Benefits

At HCLTech, we believe in empowering our employees with comprehensive benefits that support their professional growth and enhance their well-being. When you sign up for a career with us, you gain access to:

Industry-benchmarked compensation

Best-in-class healthcare benefits

Personal time off

5. Strong analytical and problem-solving skills to interpret data and provide valuable insights.
6. Excellent communication skills to collaborate with cross functional teams and stakeholders.
7. Ability to lead a team effectively, prioritize tasks, and meet project deadlines.

Other Requirements

1.Tableau Desktop Certified Professional is preferred.

description.text:
Senior Administrator - SAP BASIS
Vijayawada, Andhra Pradesh
Job Summary
The Sr. Administrator for Support & Operations plays a crucial role in delivering advanced technical support and troubleshooting expertise for SAP BASIS and SAP HANA environments. This position is essential in ensuring seamless operations, enhancing customer satisfaction, and driving continuous improvement through effective root cause analysis and resolution of complex incidents.
Key Responsibilities
1. Provide Advanced Technical Support By Adhering To Quality Standards And Regulatory Requirements While Ensuring Compliance With Company Policies In Sap Basis And Sap Hana Environments.
2. Resolve Complex Incidents Escalated By Analysts By Performing Thorough Root Cause Analysis And Implementing Effective Solutions To Address Technical And Security Issues.
3. Facilitate Knowledge Transfer By Updating And Managing The Knowledge Base, Training New Team Members, And Coaching Analysts To Enhance Team Capabilities.
4. Manage And Resolve Complex Tickets Within Agreed Slas By Collaborating With Cross-Functional Support Teams To Maintain Operational Efficiency And Ensure A Robust Security Posture.
5. Enhance The Customer Experience By Achieving High First Call Resolution Rates, Minimizing Rejected Resolutions And Case Reopenings, While Proactively Mitigating Security Threats.
Skill Requirements
1. Architecting and defining complex and technical SAP solutions across multiple industry sectors, understanding of the technology enablement as well as integration with other applications.
2. Experience of numerous SAP enabled business transformation programme lifecycles of which at least 3 of were gained in a solution architect or senior leadership role.
3. Strong knowledge of SAP solution suite and technical architecture and ability to translate business challenges
4. Strong consulting and stakeholder management skills Ã¯Â¿Â½ demonstrable presentation and client facing consulting and influencing skills up until C suite levels. All candidates must be assertive and articulate with the ability to develop and sustain senior client relationships.
5. Understanding of SAPÃ¯Â¿Â½s product strategy, including positioning of S/4 HANA and enabling tools and complementary technologies, especially the integration to other applications.
6. Good knowledge of GCP, AWS and Azure public cloud offerings and architecting

Other Requirements
1. Optional But Valuable Certifications: Sap Certified Technology Associate Sap Hana 2.0, Itil Foundation Certification

description.text:
SAP Basis Lead Consultant
Lucknow, Uttar Pradesh
Job Summary
The Lead Consultant in SAP Basis S/4HANA Upgrade & Migration is responsible for overseeing the successful upgrade and migration of SAP Basis to S/4HANA, ensuring seamless transition and optimal functionality in alignment with business objectives and requirements.
Key Responsibilities
1. Lead and manage the end-to-end upgrade and migration projects for sap basis to s/4hana.
2. Develop and execute project plans, ensuring timely delivery and quality of work.
3. Collaborate with cross functional teams to gather requirements and ensure smooth integration.
4. Conduct system assessments, troubleshooting, and performance tuning as needed.
5. Provide expertise and guidance on best practices for sap basis s/4hana configuration and optimization.
6. Perform system backups, disaster recovery, and system enhancements to support business operations.
7. Stay uptodate with the latest sap technologies and recommend solutions for continuous improvement.
Skill Requirements
1. Proficient in sap basis administration and configuration.
2. Extensive experience in sap s/4hana upgrades and migrations.
3. Strong knowledge of sap landscape transformation and system integration.
4. Ability to troubleshoot and resolve complex technical issues in sap environments.
5. Excellent project management skills with a focus on delivery and quality.
6. Strong communication and interpersonal skills to collaborate with internal teams and stakeholders effectively.
7. Certification in sap basis or s/4hana is preferred but not mandatory.

Other Requirements
1.SAP Certified Technology Associate System Administration (SAP HANA) with SAP NetWeaver 7.5 or equivalent is a plus.

description.text:
Job Details:

Senior Technical Manager, Development and Application Support (Supply Chain Management and Logistics)

Lead a global follow-the-sun application support organization along with development responsibilities for mission-critical supply chain logistics platforms. Drive stability, uptime, and SLA performance through strong operational rigor, training and accountability, reduced technical debt, and AI-enabled automation/process improvement. Partner across business and IT in a matrixed environment (including vendors) to deliver enhancements and integrations, coordinate end-to-end incident restoration with Infrastructure/Cybersecurity.

Key Responsibilities

Run global support operations to meet SLAs, improve uptime, and drive incident/problem/change management with end-to-end restoration.
Lead, coach, and develop managers and teams; set clear expectations and performance standards.
Own support strategy and roadmaps; align priorities to business outcomes and long-term platform health.
Drive continuous improvement using metrics, automation/AI, and process standardization; manage budgets/resources.
Deliver enhancements, integrations, and large-scale programs/projects with predictable agile execution.
Provide technical/architectural oversight to ensure solutions meet standards, security, and operational requirements.

Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in IT/Computer Science (or equivalent experience).
14+ years of relevant experience, including IT management and leading teams.
Relevant IT certification(s) preferred.

Core Skills

Leadership: Build accountable teams; coach leaders and drive collaboration.
Decision-making: Resolve high-pressure issues quickly; escalate strategically when needed.
Communication: Translate technical details into clear updates for non-technical stakeholders.

Essential Duties and Responsibilities:

Agile Execution: Facilitate scrum practices focusing on technical delivery, ensuring that products are built on robust, scalable models.
Broader Oversight: Manage a large team or multiple small teams, providing guidance and support to ensure operational success.
Collaborate with Senior Leadership: Collaborate with senior leadership to define IT strategy and roadmaps, aligning technological initiatives with business priorities.
Culture Driver: Foster a positive and innovative IT culture that aligns with organizational values, promotes continuous learning, and encourages collaboration across teams and departments.
Drive Strategic IT Initiatives: Drive the implementation of strategic IT initiatives aligned with organizational goals, ensuring impactful results.
Lead and Develop Teams: Lead and develop multiple teams of IT professionals across different specialties, fostering growth and collaboration.
Manage Stakeholder and Vendor Relationships: Manage relationships with key stakeholders and vendors, ensuring effective communication and successful partnerships.
Mentorship & Development: Mentor team leads and managers on advanced techniques, helping them grow their technical expertise and leadership skills.
Operations Excellence: Drive operational improvements across multiple IT teams, leverage data analytics to demonstrate effectiveness, and optimize resource allocation and budget management to ensure efficiency and cost-effectiveness.
Optimize Resource Allocation and Budgeting: Optimize resource allocation and budget management across multiple teams to ensure efficiency and cost-effectiveness.
Oversee Complex Projects: Oversee complex, large-scale projects and programs, ensuring timely delivery and adherence to quality standards.
Performance Management: Leverage the enterprise-wide performance management system to align team and individual goals with organizational objectives, mentor team leads in effective performance management practices, and ensure consistent application of performance standards across multiple teams.
Project Guidance: Guide multiple teams in implementing complex projects, ensuring they are delivered on time and meet business requirements.
Stakeholder Communication: Ensure frequent and effective communication with cross-functional teams and stakeholders to maintain alignment and transparency.
Strategic Alignment: Align IT initiatives with broader business goals and strategies, ensuring technology supports long-term business objectives.
Strategic Implementation: Ensure that IT practices are strategically implemented to support long-term business goals and objectives.
Risk Management: Develop and oversee risk management processes for multiple IT teams, proactively addressing potential issues and implementing mitigation strategies to ensure project and operational success.
Technical Oversight: Provide oversight on the architectural and design aspects of IT, ensuring alignment with industry standards and business needs.
Team Member Support and Empowerment: Actively support team members by identifying and creating opportunities for skill development, cross-functional exposure, and career advancement, ensuring their success and alignment with organizational goals.
Vendor Management: To ensure optimal service delivery, evaluate and manage relationships with IT operations vendors and service providers.
Perform other job-related duties as assigned, which align with our organization's vision, mission, and values and fall within your scope of practice.

Education: Bachelor's degree preferably in Information Technology, Computer Science, or a related field, or relevant experience.
Preferred Certification(s): Any relevant IT Certification.

Experience: 14+ years of relevant and practical experience, preferably in SAP SCM, with a proven ability to lead and motivate teams.

Special Skills:

Leadership and Team Management: Demonstrate strong leadership and team management skills, guiding teams toward achieving goals while fostering a collaborative and productive work environment.
Problem-Solving and Decision-Making: Demonstrate excellent problem-solving and decision-making skills, proactively addressing challenges and making sound decisions in high-pressure situations. Handle most operational issues autonomously, seeking upper management input primarily for strategic or high-impact decisions.
IT Project Management: Proficient in IT project management methodologies and tools, ensuring the successful planning, execution, and delivery of technology projects.
Communication and Interpersonal Skills: Strong communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to clearly explain technical concepts to non-technical staff and team members, ensuring alignment and understanding across all levels.

Relocation Assistance Eligible:

No

Work Shift:

Tyson is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will be considered without regard to race, national origin, color, religion, age, genetics, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, disability or veteran status.

description.text:
, India

Job Family Group:

Information Technology (IT)

Worker Type:

Regular

Posting Start Date:

May 4, 2026

Business Unit:

Projects and Technology

Experience Level:

Experienced Professionals

Job Description:

What’s the role

As an IT Business Analyst, you will deliver world-class business analysis services supporting application development and enhancement initiatives across Shell’s global IT landscape. Working closely with business stakeholders and clients, you will identify, analyze, and define business needs, translating them into effective solutions that address complex business challenges. You will operate within a global Information & Digital Technology (IDT) organization, collaborating with cross-functional teams across Shell’s IT hubs in Bangalore, London, Houston, and the Netherlands. The role supports the Shell Energy business, contributing to projects across Power, Gas, LNG, and Environmental Products with a strong focus on trading activities, and plays a key part in enabling Shell’s Energy transformation. You will join a collaborative, high-performing team that values technical excellence, continuous learning, and career development, offering extensive opportunities to build domain expertise, deepen Shell business knowledge, and develop leading-edge skills while delivering measurable value to the business.

What you’ll be doing

Act as a subject matter expert for the Gas or European Power businesses, bringing deep domain knowledge to solution delivery.

Lead the identification, analysis, and documentation of business requirements, partnering closely with technical teams to define feasible and effective solution options.

Manage requirements across the full end-to-end solution delivery lifecycle, including transition and knowledge handover to support teams.

Plan and deliver business analysis activities across projects, ensuring the effective use of methods, tools, and technologies.

Assure functional design quality, resolve design-related issues, and remain accountable for key solution design decisions.

Serve as the product SME for delivery teams, building credibility as a trusted advisor to business and IT stakeholders.

Work in close alignment with Business and IT leadership to shape product roadmaps, manage demand pipelines, and align capacity and capability needs.

Coach and mentor members of the wider business analysis community, contributing to the continuous uplift of domain, product, and analytical capabilities.

Provide leadership to assigned teams, supporting individual development planning and performance management.

Act as a point of escalation for issues impacting system performance, solution design, or functionality.

Apply strong analytical, leadership, and change management skills to independently drive transformation and continuous improvement initiatives, engaging effectively with senior stakeholders and vendors on complex challenges.

Translate technology concepts into clear, business-friendly language, managing expectations and adjusting scope and outcomes as requirements evolve.

Drive innovation by identifying new approaches to existing challenges and opportunities.

Build strong, trusted relationships with stakeholders by understanding their objectives, constraints, risks, and operating environments.

What you bring

Bachelor’s degree in Information Technology, Engineering, or a related discipline.

9–12 years of overall IT experience, including a minimum of 8 years in IT Business Analysis.

Strong domain experience in Power, Gas, or LNG Trading, and/or hands-on experience with ETRM systems.

Proven expertise in applying business analysis methods and tools, with a strong track record of delivering high‑quality project artefacts.

Ability to clearly articulate business problems, value drivers, and functional and non-functional requirements.

Demonstrated capability to translate business requirements into effective solutions, assessing risks, feasibility, opportunities, and business impacts across solution options.

Experience working within Agile delivery environments and collaborating with cross-functional teams.

Strong background in managing complex data sets, analysis, and data-driven decision-making.

Experience working effectively in geographically distributed, global teams.

What we offer

You bring your skills and experience to Shell and in return you work with talented, committed people on one of the most important challenges facing our planet. You’ll have the opportunity to develop the skills you need to grow in an environment where we value honesty, integrity, and respect for one another. You’ll be able to balance your priorities as you become the best version of yourself.

Progress as a person as we work on the energy transition together.

Continuously grow the transferable skills you need to get ahead.

Work at the forefront of technology, trends, and practices.

Collaborate with experienced colleagues with unique expertise.

Achieve your balance in a value-led culture that encourages you to be the best version of yourself.

Benefit from flexible working hours, and the possibility of remote/mobile working.

Perform at your best with a competitive starting salary and annual performance related salary increase – our pay and benefits packages are among the best in the world.

Take advantage of paid parental leave, including for non-birthing parents.

Join an organization to become one of the most diverse and inclusive in the world. We strongly encourage applicants of all genders, ages, ethnicities, cultures, abilities, sexual orientation, and life experiences to apply.

Grow as you progress through diverse career opportunities in national and

international teams.

Gain access to a wide range of training and development programs.

We'd like you to know that Shell has a bold goal: to become one of the world’s most diverse and inclusive companies. .

IMPORTANT NOTE: After you submit your application, you will receive an email providing a link to an online assessment that is a mandatory part of the process. The email will come from HireVue - please check your spam/junk mailbox if you do not see the email. Once completed, your application will be reviewed to determine next steps. Thank you for your interest in Shell.

-

Shell in India

Shell is a diversified energy company in India with 13,000 employees, and presence in Integrated Gas, Downstream, Power, Renewable and Upstream. Additionally, we have deep capabilities in R&D, digitalization, and business operations.

Our global strategy, Powering Progress, is designed to generate value for our shareholders, customers, and the wider society, and focuses on creating more value with less emissions. The strategy supports our purpose of providing more and cleaner energy solutions, with the aim of profitably transforming Shell into a net-zero emissions energy business by 2050. As India moves towards its target of net-zero emissions by 2070, Shell India aims to play a leading role in securing vital energy for today, while investing in, and helping to build, the energy system of the future through strategic investments in the country.

Our Lubricants business serves over 50,000 consumers through a strong network of over 200 distributors, and operates an end-to-end value chain that spans conceptualization, development, and production at a world-class blending plant at Taloja. Through our 350-plus retail stations, we offer an integrated mobility experience including fuels, cafes, and convenience stores, with a prominent network of EV recharging facilities.

Shell owns and operates an LNG re-gasification terminal at Hazira, Surat, with a capacity of 5 MTPA and an LNG truck-loading unit that plays a crucial role in helping meet India’s growing demand for gas across sectors. In 2022, Shell acquired Spring Energy in a $1.55 billion deal to build an integrated energy transition business in the country. Spring is a leading renewable energy company in India which develops and manages solar, wind, and hybrid power generation facilities and infrastructure.

Our three capability centers across Bangalore and Chennai serve as a technology and innovation powerhouse for Shell globally, working as a delivery engine for core technical, digital, and finance processes: pioneering digital innovation and cutting-edge technologies across the energy sector. We also have strong academic partnerships and collaborations with leading universities and technology institutes to accelerate decarbonization efforts within the energy sector.

We are committed to positively contributing to the communities in which we operate through programs on STEM Education, Skilling, and Livelihood across India. We nurture and invest in startups developing initiatives focused on accelerating energy innovation through programs such as Shell E4 and Shell Eco-marathon. We also have strategic investments in new energy companies such as Husk Power, d.light, Orb Energy, and Cleantech Solar.

DISCLAIMER:

Please note: We occasionally amend or withdraw Shell jobs and reserve the right to do so at any time, including prior to the advertised closing date. Before applying, you are advised to read our data protection policy. This policy describes the processing that may be associated with your personal data and informs you that your personal data may be transferred to Shell/Shell Group companies around the world. The Shell Group and its approved recruitment consultants will never ask you for a fee to process or consider your application for a career with Shell. Anyone who demands such a fee is not an authorised Shell representative and you are strongly advised to refuse any such demand. Shell is an Equal Opportunity Employer.
